Its principal deities are [[:en:Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko Ōkami]], [[:en:Ame-no-Uzume|Ame-no-Uzume]], and [[:en:Kukurihime|Kukuri-hime-no-Mikoto]].
== History == According to shrine tradition, it was founded on November 25 in the 25th year of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]], and during the reign of [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]], it became a branch shrine of [[:en:Ikuta Shrine|Ikuta Shrine]]. In the 2nd year of [[:en:Daidō|Daido]] ([[:en:807|807 AD]]), a shrine building was constructed, and during the [[:en:Kōhei|Kōhei Era]] ([[:en:1058|1058 AD]] – [[:en:1065|1065 AD]]), [[:en:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] donated [[:en:Kanda|Kanda]]. Since the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], the warrior families ({{ill|Buke (Japan)|de|Kriegerfamilien|ja|武家|qq=draft|lt=Buke|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buke (Japan)|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) have shown strong devotion, and it is said that [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] also made donations, with {{ill|Ashikaga Harushi|de|Ashikaga Harushi|ja|足利晴氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashikaga Harushi|qq=}} having offered a golden seal that still exists today.
In the 9th year of the [[:en:Eiroku|Eiroku Era]] ([[:en:1566|1566 AD]]), the shrine building was burned down in a battle between the {{ill|Kaijō Clan|de|Kaijō-Clan|ja|海上氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaijō Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the {{ill|Awa Masaki Clan|de|Awa Masaki-Clan|ja|安房正木氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awa Masaki Clan|qq=}}. Although it was rebuilt during the [[:en:Genki (era)|Genki Era]], it was again burned down in the 1st year of the [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] ([[:en:1573|1573 AD]]) and rebuilt in the following 2nd year of Tenshō ([[:en:1574|1574 AD]]). The current main hall was reconstructed in the 8th year of the [[:en:Enpō|Enpō Era]] ([[:en:1680|1680 AD]]). Additionally, in the 19th year of Tenshō ([[:en:1591|1591 AD]]), [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] donated 30 stones of {{ill|Red Seal Land|de|Rotsiegel-Land|ja|朱印地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Red Seal Land|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 朱印地・黒印地}}.
Every 60 years, in the {{ill|Kōshin Year|de|Kōshin-Jahr|ja|庚申|1=Kōshin|lt=Yang Metal Monkey|2=ja|3=庚申|4=zh|5=庚申|6=ko|7=경신|lt_ko=경신|8=fr|9=Singe de métal|10=de|11=Metall-Affe|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin Year|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, the Shikinen Taisai Kamiyuki-sai is held.
== Cultural Properties == * One plaque from the annex of the Saruta Shrine main hall ({{ill|Chiba Prefecture-designated Tangible Cultural Property|de|Chiba-Präfektur-designierte greifbare Kulturimmobilie|ja|千葉県指定文化財一覧#建造物|lt=Chiba Prefecture-designated Tangible Cultural Property|lt_ja=千葉県指定有形文化財|lt_de=Chiba-Präfektur-designierte greifbare Kulturimmobilie|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiba Prefecture-designated Tangible Cultural Property|qq=}}) * The forest of Saruta Shrine ({{ill|Chiba Prefecture-designated Natural Monument|de|Chiba-Präfektur-designiertes Naturdenkmal|ja|千葉県指定文化財一覧#天然記念物|lt=Chiba Prefecture-designated Natural Monument|lt_ja=千葉県指定天然記念物|lt_de=Chiba-Präfektur-designiertes Naturdenkmal|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiba Prefecture-designated Natural Monument|qq=}})
== Access == * 5-minute walk from [[:en:Saruda Station|Saruta Station]] on the [[:en:Sōbu Main Line|Sōbu Main Line]]. * From either [[:en:Japan National Route 126|National Route 126]] at Asahi City's Ato or [[:en:Japan National Route 356|National Route 356]] at Choshi City's Nojiri, via the {{ill|Choshi-Asahi Line (Chiba Prefectural Road 71)|de|Choshi-Asahi-Linie (Chiba-Prefekturstraße 71)|ja|千葉県道71号銚子旭線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Choshi-Asahi Line (Chiba Prefectural Road 71)|qq=}}.
== Other == * There is a bridge called Sakushinbashi on the approach road that spans the JR [[:en:Sōbu Main Line|Sōbu Main Line]]. It was constructed by the railway company when the {{ill|Sōbu Railway (first generation)|de|Sōbu-Eisenbahn (Erste Generation)|ja|総武鉄道 (初代)|lt=Sōbu Railway|lt_ja=総武鉄道|lt_de=Sōbu Eisenbahn|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōbu Railway (first generation)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 総武本線}} was extended to [[:en:Chōshi Station|Choshi Station]] in [[:en:1897|1897 AD]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 30), which resulted in the approach road being divided. * Due to the remnants of [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]], the shrine office is closed on Butsumetsu ({{ill|Butsumetsu (Buddhist calendar)|de|Butsumetsu|ja|六曜#仏滅|lt=Butsumetsu|lt_ja=仏滅|lt_de=Butsumetsu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Butsumetsu (Buddhist calendar)|qq=}}) day.
== References == {{No footnotes|date=2020年2月|section=1}} * 『National Shrine Directory, Volume 1』, National Shrine Directory Publishing Society History Center, Showa 52. * 『Kadokawa Great Dictionary of Japanese Place Names, Volume 12: Chiba Prefecture』, Kadokawa Great Dictionary of Japanese Place Names Editorial Committee, Showa 59.
== External Links == * [http://www.choshikanko.com/extra/saruta/ Choshi City Saruta Shrine]

'''Saruta Shrine''' (猿田神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shrine]] located in Saruta-machi, [[:en:Chōshi|Choshi City]] of [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]]. It has long been venerated for practices such as {{ill|Directional Deity|de|Richtgott|ja|方位神|1=Directional deity|lt=Eight-Directional Protection|lt_ja=八方除け|lt_de=Achtseitiger Schutz|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Directional Deity|13=User:Immanuelle/Directional deity|qq=}}. Its principal deities are [[:en:Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko Ōkami]], [[:en:Ame-no-Uzume|Ame-no-Uzume]], and [[:en:Kukurihime|Kukuri-hime-no-Mikoto]].
== History == According to shrine tradition, it was founded on November 25 in the 25th year of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]], and during the reign of [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]], it became a branch shrine of [[:en:Ikuta Shrine|Ikuta Shrine]]. In the 2nd year of [[:en:Daidō|Daido]] ([[:en:807|807 AD]]), a shrine building was constructed, and during the [[:en:Kōhei|Kōhei Era]] ([[:en:1058|1058 AD]] – [[:en:1065|1065 AD]]), [[:en:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] donated [[:en:Kanda|Kanda]]. Since the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], the warrior families ({{ill|Buke (Japan)|de|Kriegerfamilien|ja|武家|qq=draft|lt=Buke|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buke (Japan)|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) have shown strong devotion, and it is said that [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] also made donations, with {{ill|Ashikaga Harushi|de|Ashikaga Harushi|ja|足利晴氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashikaga Harushi|qq=}} having offered a golden seal that still exists today.
In the 9th year of the [[:en:Eiroku|Eiroku Era]] ([[:en:1566|1566 AD]]), the shrine building was burned down in a battle between the {{ill|Kaijō Clan|de|Kaijō-Clan|ja|海上氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaijō Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the {{ill|Awa Masaki Clan|de|Awa Masaki-Clan|ja|安房正木氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awa Masaki Clan|qq=}}. Although it was rebuilt during the [[:en:Genki (era)|Genki Era]], it was again burned down in the 1st year of the [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] ([[:en:1573|1573 AD]]) and rebuilt in the following 2nd year of Tenshō ([[:en:1574|1574 AD]]). The current main hall was reconstructed in the 8th year of the [[:en:Enpō|Enpō Era]] ([[:en:1680|1680 AD]]). Additionally, in the 19th year of Tenshō ([[:en:1591|1591 AD]]), [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] donated 30 stones of {{ill|Red Seal Land|de|Rotsiegel-Land|ja|朱印地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Red Seal Land|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 朱印地・黒印地}}.
Every 60 years, in the {{ill|Kōshin Year|de|Kōshin-Jahr|ja|庚申|1=Kōshin|lt=Yang Metal Monkey|2=ja|3=庚申|4=zh|5=庚申|6=ko|7=경신|lt_ko=경신|8=fr|9=Singe de métal|10=de|11=Metall-Affe|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin Year|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, the Shikinen Taisai Kamiyuki-sai is held.
== Cultural Properties == * One plaque from the annex of the Saruta Shrine main hall ({{ill|Chiba Prefecture-designated Tangible Cultural Property|de|Chiba-Präfektur-designierte greifbare Kulturimmobilie|ja|千葉県指定文化財一覧#建造物|lt=Chiba Prefecture-designated Tangible Cultural Property|lt_ja=千葉県指定有形文化財|lt_de=Chiba-Präfektur-designierte greifbare Kulturimmobilie|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiba Prefecture-designated Tangible Cultural Property|qq=}}) * The forest of Saruta Shrine ({{ill|Chiba Prefecture-designated Natural Monument|de|Chiba-Präfektur-designiertes Naturdenkmal|ja|千葉県指定文化財一覧#天然記念物|lt=Chiba Prefecture-designated Natural Monument|lt_ja=千葉県指定天然記念物|lt_de=Chiba-Präfektur-designiertes Naturdenkmal|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiba Prefecture-designated Natural Monument|qq=}})

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11572147]] Wikidata name: Saruda-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:07, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name= Saruta Shrine |image= File:Saruda-jinjya-shrine,choshi-city,japan.JPG |Location= [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]] [[:en:Chōshi|Choshi City]] Saruta-machi 1677 |Coordinates= {{WikiCoordinatesTwoTierDMS|35|44|50|N|140|44|0|E|scale:25000}} |Enshrined Deities= [[:en:Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko Ōkami]]・[[:en:Ame-no-Uzume|Ame-no-Uzume]]・[[:en:Kukurihime|Kukuri-hime-no-Mikoto]] |Shrine Rank= Unranked Shrine |Established= 25th year of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]] |Main Hall= Nagare-zukuri |Alternate Name= |Annual Festival= November 15 |Sacred Rites= Shikinen Taisai Kamiyuki-sai |prefecture= {{ill|Chōshi|qid=Q694317}} |coordinates= {{Coord|35.747222|N|140.733333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity= {{ill|Ama-no-Uzume|qid=Q462803}}; {{ill|Kukurihime|qid=Q11619891}}; {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|qid=Q3090037}} }}
'''Saruta Shrine''' (猿田神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shrine]] located in Saruta-machi, [[:en:Chōshi|Choshi City]] of [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]]. It has long been venerated for practices such as {{ill|Directional Deity|de|Richtgott|ja|方位神|1=Directional deity|lt=Eight-Directional Protection|lt_ja=八方除け|lt_de=Achtseitiger Schutz|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Directional Deity|13=User:Immanuelle/Directional deity|qq=}}. Its principal deities are [[:en:Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko Ōkami]], [[:en:Ame-no-Uzume|Ame-no-Uzume]], and [[:en:Kukurihime|Kukuri-hime-no-Mikoto]].
== History == According to shrine tradition, it was founded on November 25 in the 25th year of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]], and during the reign of [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]], it became a branch shrine of [[:en:Ikuta Shrine|Ikuta Shrine]]. In the 2nd year of [[:en:Daidō|Daido]] ([[:en:807|807 AD]]), a shrine building was constructed, and during the [[:en:Kōhei|Kōhei Era]] ([[:en:1058|1058 AD]] – [[:en:1065|1065 AD]]), [[:en:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] donated [[:en:Kanda|Kanda]]. Since the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], the warrior families ({{ill|Buke (Japan)|de|Kriegerfamilien|ja|武家|qq=draft|lt=Buke|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buke (Japan)|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) have shown strong devotion, and it is said that [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] also made donations, with {{ill|Ashikaga Harushi|de|Ashikaga Harushi|ja|足利晴氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashikaga Harushi|qq=}} having offered a golden seal that still exists today.
In the 9th year of the [[:en:Eiroku|Eiroku Era]] ([[:en:1566|1566 AD]]), the shrine building was burned down in a battle between the {{ill|Kaijō Clan|de|Kaijō-Clan|ja|海上氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaijō Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the {{ill|Awa Masaki Clan|de|Awa Masaki-Clan|ja|安房正木氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awa Masaki Clan|qq=}}. Although it was rebuilt during the [[:en:Genki (era)|Genki Era]], it was again burned down in the 1st year of the [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] ([[:en:1573|1573 AD]]) and rebuilt in the following 2nd year of Tenshō ([[:en:1574|1574 AD]]). The current main hall was reconstructed in the 8th year of the [[:en:Enpō|Enpō Era]] ([[:en:1680|1680 AD]]). Additionally, in the 19th year of Tenshō ([[:en:1591|1591 AD]]), [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] donated 30 stones of {{ill|Red Seal Land|de|Rotsiegel-Land|ja|朱印地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Red Seal Land|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 朱印地・黒印地}}.
Every 60 years, in the {{ill|Kōshin Year|de|Kōshin-Jahr|ja|庚申|1=Kōshin|lt=Yang Metal Monkey|2=ja|3=庚申|4=zh|5=庚申|6=ko|7=경신|lt_ko=경신|8=fr|9=Singe de métal|10=de|11=Metall-Affe|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin Year|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, the Shikinen Taisai Kamiyuki-sai is held.
== Cultural Properties == * One plaque from the annex of the Saruta Shrine main hall ({{ill|Chiba Prefecture-designated Tangible Cultural Property|de|Chiba-Präfektur-designierte greifbare Kulturimmobilie|ja|千葉県指定文化財一覧#建造物|lt=Chiba Prefecture-designated Tangible Cultural Property|lt_ja=千葉県指定有形文化財|lt_de=Chiba-Präfektur-designierte greifbare Kulturimmobilie|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiba Prefecture-designated Tangible Cultural Property|qq=}}) * The forest of Saruta Shrine ({{ill|Chiba Prefecture-designated Natural Monument|de|Chiba-Präfektur-designiertes Naturdenkmal|ja|千葉県指定文化財一覧#天然記念物|lt=Chiba Prefecture-designated Natural Monument|lt_ja=千葉県指定天然記念物|lt_de=Chiba-Präfektur-designiertes Naturdenkmal|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiba Prefecture-designated Natural Monument|qq=}})
== Access == * 5-minute walk from [[:en:Saruda Station|Saruta Station]] on the [[:en:Sōbu Main Line|Sōbu Main Line]]. * From either [[:en:Japan National Route 126|National Route 126]] at Asahi City's Ato or [[:en:Japan National Route 356|National Route 356]] at Choshi City's Nojiri, via the {{ill|Choshi-Asahi Line (Chiba Prefectural Road 71)|de|Choshi-Asahi-Linie (Chiba-Prefekturstraße 71)|ja|千葉県道71号銚子旭線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Choshi-Asahi Line (Chiba Prefectural Road 71)|qq=}}.
== Other == * There is a bridge called Sakushinbashi on the approach road that spans the JR [[:en:Sōbu Main Line|Sōbu Main Line]]. It was constructed by the railway company when the {{ill|Sōbu Railway (first generation)|de|Sōbu-Eisenbahn (Erste Generation)|ja|総武鉄道 (初代)|lt=Sōbu Railway|lt_ja=総武鉄道|lt_de=Sōbu Eisenbahn|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōbu Railway (first generation)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 総武本線}} was extended to [[:en:Chōshi Station|Choshi Station]] in [[:en:1897|1897 AD]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 30), which resulted in the approach road being divided. * Due to the remnants of [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]], the shrine office is closed on Butsumetsu ({{ill|Butsumetsu (Buddhist calendar)|de|Butsumetsu|ja|六曜#仏滅|lt=Butsumetsu|lt_ja=仏滅|lt_de=Butsumetsu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Butsumetsu (Buddhist calendar)|qq=}}) day.
== References == {{No footnotes|date=2020年2月|section=1}} * 『National Shrine Directory, Volume 1』, National Shrine Directory Publishing Society History Center, Showa 52. * 『Kadokawa Great Dictionary of Japanese Place Names, Volume 12: Chiba Prefecture』, Kadokawa Great Dictionary of Japanese Place Names Editorial Committee, Showa 59.
== External Links == * [http://www.choshikanko.com/extra/saruta/ Choshi City Saruta Shrine]

Every 60 years, in the {{ill|Kōshin Year|de|Kōshin-Jahr|ja|庚申|1=Kōshin|lt=Yang Metal Monkey|2=ja|3=庚申|4=zh|5=庚申|6=ko|7=경신|lt_ko=경신|8=fr|9=Singe de métal|10=de|11=Metall-Affe|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin Year|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, the Shikinen Taisai Kamiyuki-sai is held.
== Cultural Properties == * One plaque from the annex of the Saruta Shrine main hall ({{ill|Chiba Prefecture-designated Tangible Cultural Property|de|Chiba-Präfektur-designierte greifbare Kulturimmobilie|ja|千葉県指定文化財一覧#建造物|lt=Chiba Prefecture-designated Tangible Cultural Property|lt_ja=千葉県指定有形文化財|lt_de=Chiba-Präfektur-designierte greifbare Kulturimmobilie|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiba Prefecture-designated Tangible Cultural Property|qq=|1=Chiba Prefecture-designated Tangible Cultural Property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406175}}) * The forest of Saruta Shrine ({{ill|Chiba Prefecture-designated Natural Monument|de|Chiba-Präfektur-designiertes Naturdenkmal|ja|千葉県指定文化財一覧#天然記念物|lt=Chiba Prefecture-designated Natural Monument|lt_ja=千葉県指定天然記念物|lt_de=Chiba-Präfektur-designiertes Naturdenkmal|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiba Prefecture-designated Natural Monument|qq=|1=Chiba Prefecture-designated Natural Monument|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406175}})
== Access == * 5-minute walk from [[:en:Saruda Station|Saruta Station]] on the [[:en:Sōbu Main Line|Sōbu Main Line]]. * From either [[:en:Japan National Route 126|National Route 126]] at Asahi City's Ato or [[:en:Japan National Route 356|National Route 356]] at Choshi City's Nojiri, via the {{ill|Choshi-Asahi Line (Chiba Prefectural Road 71)|de|Choshi-Asahi-Linie (Chiba-Prefekturstraße 71)|ja|千葉県道71号銚子旭線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Choshi-Asahi Line (Chiba Prefectural Road 71)|qq=|1=Chiba Prefectural Road Route 71|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406653}}.
== Other == * There is a bridge called Sakushinbashi on the approach road that spans the JR [[:en:Sōbu Main Line|Sōbu Main Line]]. It was constructed by the railway company when the {{ill|Sōbu Railway (first generation)|de|Sōbu-Eisenbahn (Erste Generation)|ja|総武鉄道 (初代)|lt=Sōbu Railway|lt_ja=総武鉄道|lt_de=Sōbu Eisenbahn|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōbu Railway (first generation)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 総武本線|1=Sōbu Main Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1191364}} was extended to [[:en:Chōshi Station|Choshi Station]] in [[:en:1897|1897 AD]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 30), which resulted in the approach road being divided. * Due to the remnants of [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]], the shrine office is closed on Butsumetsu ({{ill|Butsumetsu (Buddhist calendar)|de|Butsumetsu|ja|六曜#仏滅|lt=Butsumetsu|lt_ja=仏滅|lt_de=Butsumetsu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Butsumetsu (Buddhist calendar)|qq=|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}) day.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:猿田神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11572147]] Wikidata name: Saruda-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:07, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name= Saruta Shrine |image= File:Saruda-jinjya-shrine,choshi-city,japan.JPG |Location= [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]] [[:en:Chōshi|Choshi City]] Saruta-machi 1677 |Coordinates= {{WikiCoordinatesTwoTierDMS|35|44|50|N|140|44|0|E|scale:25000}} |Enshrined Deities= [[:en:Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko Ōkami]]・[[:en:Ame-no-Uzume|Ame-no-Uzume]]・[[:en:Kukurihime|Kukuri-hime-no-Mikoto]] |Shrine Rank= Unranked Shrine |Established= 25th year of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]] |Main Hall= Nagare-zukuri |Alternate Name= |Annual Festival= November 15 |Sacred Rites= Shikinen Taisai Kamiyuki-sai |prefecture= {{ill|Chōshi|WD=Q694317}} |coordinates= {{Coord|35.747222|N|140.733333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity= {{ill|Ama-no-Uzume|WD=Q462803}}; {{ill|Kukurihime|WD=Q11619891}}; {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|WD=Q3090037}} }}
'''Saruta Shrine''' (猿田神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shrine]] located in Saruta-machi, [[:en:Chōshi|Choshi City]] of [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]]. It has long been venerated for practices such as {{ill|Directional Deity|de|Richtgott|ja|方位神|1=Directional deity|lt=Eight-Directional Protection|lt_ja=八方除け|lt_de=Achtseitiger Schutz|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Directional Deity|13=User:Immanuelle/Directional deity|qq=}}. Its principal deities are [[:en:Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko Ōkami]], [[:en:Ame-no-Uzume|Ame-no-Uzume]], and [[:en:Kukurihime|Kukuri-hime-no-Mikoto]].
== History == According to shrine tradition, it was founded on November 25 in the 25th year of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]], and during the reign of [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]], it became a branch shrine of [[:en:Ikuta Shrine|Ikuta Shrine]]. In the 2nd year of [[:en:Daidō|Daido]] ([[:en:807|807 AD]]), a shrine building was constructed, and during the [[:en:Kōhei|Kōhei Era]] ([[:en:1058|1058 AD]] – [[:en:1065|1065 AD]]), [[:en:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] donated [[:en:Kanda|Kanda]]. Since the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], the warrior families ({{ill|Buke (Japan)|de|Kriegerfamilien|ja|武家|qq=draft|lt=Buke|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buke (Japan)|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=buke|WD=Q2927809}}) have shown strong devotion, and it is said that [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] also made donations, with {{ill|Ashikaga Harushi|de|Ashikaga Harushi|ja|足利晴氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashikaga Harushi|qq=|1=Ashikaga Haruuji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11256588}} having offered a golden seal that still exists today.
In the 9th year of the [[:en:Eiroku|Eiroku Era]] ([[:en:1566|1566 AD]]), the shrine building was burned down in a battle between the {{ill|Kaijō Clan|de|Kaijō-Clan|ja|海上氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaijō Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the {{ill|Awa Masaki Clan|de|Awa Masaki-Clan|ja|安房正木氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awa Masaki Clan|qq=|1=Awa Masaki Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450720}}. Although it was rebuilt during the [[:en:Genki (era)|Genki Era]], it was again burned down in the 1st year of the [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] ([[:en:1573|1573 AD]]) and rebuilt in the following 2nd year of Tenshō ([[:en:1574|1574 AD]]). The current main hall was reconstructed in the 8th year of the [[:en:Enpō|Enpō Era]] ([[:en:1680|1680 AD]]). Additionally, in the 19th year of Tenshō ([[:en:1591|1591 AD]]), [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] donated 30 stones of {{ill|Red Seal Land|de|Rotsiegel-Land|ja|朱印地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Red Seal Land|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 朱印地・黒印地|1=shuinchi and kokuinchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11521520}}.
Every 60 years, in the {{ill|Kōshin Year|de|Kōshin-Jahr|ja|庚申|1=Kōshin|lt=Yang Metal Monkey|2=ja|3=庚申|4=zh|5=庚申|6=ko|7=경신|lt_ko=경신|8=fr|9=Singe de métal|10=de|11=Metall-Affe|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin Year|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, the Shikinen Taisai Kamiyuki-sai is held.
== Cultural Properties == * One plaque from the annex of the Saruta Shrine main hall ({{ill|Chiba Prefecture-designated Tangible Cultural Property|de|Chiba-Präfektur-designierte greifbare Kulturimmobilie|ja|千葉県指定文化財一覧#建造物|lt=Chiba Prefecture-designated Tangible Cultural Property|lt_ja=千葉県指定有形文化財|lt_de=Chiba-Präfektur-designierte greifbare Kulturimmobilie|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiba Prefecture-designated Tangible Cultural Property|qq=|1=Chiba Prefecture-designated Tangible Cultural Property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406175}}) * The forest of Saruta Shrine ({{ill|Chiba Prefecture-designated Natural Monument|de|Chiba-Präfektur-designiertes Naturdenkmal|ja|千葉県指定文化財一覧#天然記念物|lt=Chiba Prefecture-designated Natural Monument|lt_ja=千葉県指定天然記念物|lt_de=Chiba-Präfektur-designiertes Naturdenkmal|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiba Prefecture-designated Natural Monument|qq=|1=Chiba Prefecture-designated Natural Monument|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406175}})
== Access == * 5-minute walk from [[:en:Saruda Station|Saruta Station]] on the [[:en:Sōbu Main Line|Sōbu Main Line]]. * From either [[:en:Japan National Route 126|National Route 126]] at Asahi City's Ato or [[:en:Japan National Route 356|National Route 356]] at Choshi City's Nojiri, via the {{ill|Choshi-Asahi Line (Chiba Prefectural Road 71)|de|Choshi-Asahi-Linie (Chiba-Prefekturstraße 71)|ja|千葉県道71号銚子旭線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Choshi-Asahi Line (Chiba Prefectural Road 71)|qq=|1=Chiba Prefectural Road Route 71|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406653}}.
== Other == * There is a bridge called Sakushinbashi on the approach road that spans the JR [[:en:Sōbu Main Line|Sōbu Main Line]]. It was constructed by the railway company when the {{ill|Sōbu Railway (first generation)|de|Sōbu-Eisenbahn (Erste Generation)|ja|総武鉄道 (初代)|lt=Sōbu Railway|lt_ja=総武鉄道|lt_de=Sōbu Eisenbahn|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōbu Railway (first generation)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 総武本線|1=Sōbu Main Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1191364}} was extended to [[:en:Chōshi Station|Choshi Station]] in [[:en:1897|1897 AD]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 30), which resulted in the approach road being divided. * Due to the remnants of [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]], the shrine office is closed on Butsumetsu ({{ill|Butsumetsu (Buddhist calendar)|de|Butsumetsu|ja|六曜#仏滅|lt=Butsumetsu|lt_ja=仏滅|lt_de=Butsumetsu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Butsumetsu (Buddhist calendar)|qq=|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}) day.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:猿田神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11572147]] Wikidata name: Saruda-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:07, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name= Saruta Shrine |image= File:Saruda-jinjya-shrine,choshi-city,japan.JPG |Location= [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]] [[:en:Chōshi|Choshi City]] Saruta-machi 1677 |Coordinates= {{WikiCoordinatesTwoTierDMS|35|44|50|N|140|44|0|E|scale:25000}} |Enshrined Deities= [[:en:Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko Ōkami]]・[[:en:Ame-no-Uzume|Ame-no-Uzume]]・[[:en:Kukurihime|Kukuri-hime-no-Mikoto]] |Shrine Rank= Unranked Shrine |Established= 25th year of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]] |Main Hall= Nagare-zukuri |Alternate Name= |Annual Festival= November 15 |Sacred Rites= Shikinen Taisai Kamiyuki-sai |prefecture= {{ill|Chōshi|WD=Q694317}} |coordinates= {{Coord|35.747222|N|140.733333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity= {{ill|Ama-no-Uzume|WD=Q462803}}; {{ill|Kukurihime|WD=Q11619891}}; {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|WD=Q3090037}} }}
'''Saruta Shrine''' (猿田神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shrine]] located in Saruta-machi, [[:en:Chōshi|Choshi City]] of [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]]. It has long been venerated for practices such as {{ill|Directional Deity|de|Richtgott|ja|方位神|1=Directional deity|lt=Eight-Directional Protection|lt_ja=八方除け|lt_de=Achtseitiger Schutz|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Directional Deity|13=User:Immanuelle/Directional deity|qq=}}. Its principal deities are [[:en:Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko Ōkami]], [[:en:Ame-no-Uzume|Ame-no-Uzume]], and [[:en:Kukurihime|Kukuri-hime-no-Mikoto]].
== History == According to shrine tradition, it was founded on November 25 in the 25th year of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]], and during the reign of [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]], it became a branch shrine of [[:en:Ikuta Shrine|Ikuta Shrine]]. In the 2nd year of [[:en:Daidō|Daido]] ([[:en:807|807 AD]]), a shrine building was constructed, and during the [[:en:Kōhei|Kōhei Era]] ([[:en:1058|1058 AD]] – [[:en:1065|1065 AD]]), [[:en:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] donated [[:en:Kanda|Kanda]]. Since the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], the warrior families ({{ill|Buke (Japan)|de|Kriegerfamilien|ja|武家|qq=draft|lt=Buke|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buke (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=buke|WD=Q2927809}}) have shown strong devotion, and it is said that [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] also made donations, with {{ill|Ashikaga Harushi|de|Ashikaga Harushi|ja|足利晴氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashikaga Harushi|1=Ashikaga Haruuji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11256588}} having offered a golden seal that still exists today.
In the 9th year of the [[:en:Eiroku|Eiroku Era]] ([[:en:1566|1566 AD]]), the shrine building was burned down in a battle between the {{ill|Kaijō Clan|de|Kaijō-Clan|ja|海上氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaijō Clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the {{ill|Awa Masaki Clan|de|Awa Masaki-Clan|ja|安房正木氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awa Masaki Clan|1=Awa Masaki Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450720}}. Although it was rebuilt during the [[:en:Genki (era)|Genki Era]], it was again burned down in the 1st year of the [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] ([[:en:1573|1573 AD]]) and rebuilt in the following 2nd year of Tenshō ([[:en:1574|1574 AD]]). The current main hall was reconstructed in the 8th year of the [[:en:Enpō|Enpō Era]] ([[:en:1680|1680 AD]]). Additionally, in the 19th year of Tenshō ([[:en:1591|1591 AD]]), [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] donated 30 stones of {{ill|Red Seal Land|de|Rotsiegel-Land|ja|朱印地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Red Seal Land|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 朱印地・黒印地|1=shuinchi and kokuinchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11521520}}.
Every 60 years, in the {{ill|Kōshin Year|de|Kōshin-Jahr|ja|庚申|1=Kōshin|lt=Yang Metal Monkey|2=ja|3=庚申|4=zh|5=庚申|6=ko|7=경신|lt_ko=경신|8=fr|9=Singe de métal|10=de|11=Metall-Affe|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin Year|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, the Shikinen Taisai Kamiyuki-sai is held.
== Cultural Properties == * One plaque from the annex of the Saruta Shrine main hall ({{ill|Chiba Prefecture-designated Tangible Cultural Property|de|Chiba-Präfektur-designierte greifbare Kulturimmobilie|ja|千葉県指定文化財一覧#建造物|lt=Chiba Prefecture-designated Tangible Cultural Property|lt_ja=千葉県指定有形文化財|lt_de=Chiba-Präfektur-designierte greifbare Kulturimmobilie|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiba Prefecture-designated Tangible Cultural Property|1=Chiba Prefecture-designated Tangible Cultural Property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406175}}) * The forest of Saruta Shrine ({{ill|Chiba Prefecture-designated Natural Monument|de|Chiba-Präfektur-designiertes Naturdenkmal|ja|千葉県指定文化財一覧#天然記念物|lt=Chiba Prefecture-designated Natural Monument|lt_ja=千葉県指定天然記念物|lt_de=Chiba-Präfektur-designiertes Naturdenkmal|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiba Prefecture-designated Natural Monument|1=Chiba Prefecture-designated Natural Monument|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406175}})
== Access == * 5-minute walk from [[:en:Saruda Station|Saruta Station]] on the [[:en:Sōbu Main Line|Sōbu Main Line]]. * From either [[:en:Japan National Route 126|National Route 126]] at Asahi City's Ato or [[:en:Japan National Route 356|National Route 356]] at Choshi City's Nojiri, via the {{ill|Choshi-Asahi Line (Chiba Prefectural Road 71)|de|Choshi-Asahi-Linie (Chiba-Prefekturstraße 71)|ja|千葉県道71号銚子旭線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Choshi-Asahi Line (Chiba Prefectural Road 71)|1=Chiba Prefectural Road Route 71|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406653}}.
== Other == * There is a bridge called Sakushinbashi on the approach road that spans the JR [[:en:Sōbu Main Line|Sōbu Main Line]]. It was constructed by the railway company when the {{ill|Sōbu Railway (first generation)|de|Sōbu-Eisenbahn (Erste Generation)|ja|総武鉄道 (初代)|lt=Sōbu Railway|lt_ja=総武鉄道|lt_de=Sōbu Eisenbahn|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōbu Railway (first generation)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 総武本線|1=Sōbu Main Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1191364}} was extended to [[:en:Chōshi Station|Choshi Station]] in [[:en:1897|1897 AD]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 30), which resulted in the approach road being divided. * Due to the remnants of [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]], the shrine office is closed on Butsumetsu ({{ill|Butsumetsu (Buddhist calendar)|de|Butsumetsu|ja|六曜#仏滅|lt=Butsumetsu|lt_ja=仏滅|lt_de=Butsumetsu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Butsumetsu (Buddhist calendar)|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}) day.
== References == {{No footnotes|date=2020年2月|section=1}} * 『National Shrine Directory, Volume 1』, National Shrine Directory Publishing Society History Center, Showa 52. * 『Kadokawa Great Dictionary of Japanese Place Names, Volume 12: Chiba Prefecture』, Kadokawa Great Dictionary of Japanese Place Names Editorial Committee, Showa 59.
== External Links == * [http://www.choshikanko.com/extra/saruta/ Choshi City Saruta Shrine]

== Cultural Properties == * One plaque from the annex of the Saruta Shrine main hall ({{ill|Chiba Prefecture-designated Tangible Cultural Property|de|Chiba-Präfektur-designierte greifbare Kulturimmobilie|ja|千葉県指定文化財一覧#建造物|lt=Chiba Prefecture-designated Tangible Cultural Property|lt_ja=千葉県指定有形文化財|lt_de=Chiba-Präfektur-designierte greifbare Kulturimmobilie|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiba Prefecture-designated Tangible Cultural Property|1=Chiba Prefecture-designated Tangible Cultural Property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406175}}) * The forest of Saruta Shrine ({{ill|Chiba Prefecture-designated Natural Monument|de|Chiba-Präfektur-designiertes Naturdenkmal|ja|千葉県指定文化財一覧#天然記念物|lt=Chiba Prefecture-designated Natural Monument|lt_ja=千葉県指定天然記念物|lt_de=Chiba-Präfektur-designiertes Naturdenkmal|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiba Prefecture-designated Natural Monument|1=Chiba Prefecture-designated Natural Monument|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406175}})
== Access == * 5-minute walk from [[:en:Saruda Station|Saruta Station]] on the [[:en:Sōbu Main Line|Sōbu Main Line]]. * From either [[:en:Japan National Route 126|National Route 126]] at Asahi City's Ato or [[:en:Japan National Route 356|National Route 356]] at Choshi City's Nojiri, via the {{ill|Choshi-Asahi Line (Chiba Prefectural Road 71)|de|Choshi-Asahi-Linie (Chiba-Prefekturstraße 71)|ja|千葉県道71号銚子旭線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Choshi-Asahi Line (Chiba Prefectural Road 71)|1=Chiba Prefectural Road Route 71|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406653}}.
== Other == * There is a bridge called Sakushinbashi on the approach road that spans the JR [[:en:Sōbu Main Line|Sōbu Main Line]]. It was constructed by the railway company when the {{ill|Sōbu Railway (first generation)|de|Sōbu-Eisenbahn (Erste Generation)|ja|総武鉄道 (初代)|lt=Sōbu Railway|lt_ja=総武鉄道|lt_de=Sōbu Eisenbahn|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōbu Railway (first generation)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 総武本線|1=Sōbu Main Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1191364}} was extended to [[:en:Chōshi Station|Choshi Station]] in [[:en:1897|1897 AD]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 30), which resulted in the approach road being divided. * Due to the remnants of [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]], the shrine office is closed on Butsumetsu ({{ill|Butsumetsu (Buddhist calendar)|de|Butsumetsu|ja|六曜#仏滅|lt=Butsumetsu|lt_ja=仏滅|lt_de=Butsumetsu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Butsumetsu (Buddhist calendar)|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}) day.
== References == {{No footnotes|date=2020年2月|section=1}} * 『National Shrine Directory, Volume 1』, National Shrine Directory Publishing Society History Center, Showa 52. * 『Kadokawa Great Dictionary of Japanese Place Names, Volume 12: Chiba Prefecture』, Kadokawa Great Dictionary of Japanese Place Names Editorial Committee, Showa 59.
== External Links == * [http://www.choshikanko.com/extra/saruta/ Choshi City Saruta Shrine]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:猿田神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11572147]] Wikidata name: Saruda-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:07, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name= Saruta Shrine |image= File:Saruda-jinjya-shrine,choshi-city,japan.JPG |Location= [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]] [[:en:Chōshi|Choshi City]] Saruta-machi 1677 |Coordinates= {{WikiCoordinatesTwoTierDMS|35|44|50|N|140|44|0|E|scale:25000}} |Enshrined Deities= [[:en:Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko Ōkami]]・[[:en:Ame-no-Uzume|Ame-no-Uzume]]・[[:en:Kukurihime|Kukuri-hime-no-Mikoto]] |Shrine Rank= Unranked Shrine |Established= 25th year of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]] |Main Hall= Nagare-zukuri |Alternate Name= |Annual Festival= November 15 |Sacred Rites= Shikinen Taisai Kamiyuki-sai |prefecture= {{ill|Chōshi|WD=Q694317}} |coordinates= {{Coord|35.747222|N|140.733333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity= {{ill|Ama-no-Uzume|WD=Q462803}}; {{ill|Kukurihime|WD=Q11619891}}; {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|WD=Q3090037}} }}
'''Saruta Shrine''' (猿田神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shrine]] located in Saruta-machi, [[:en:Chōshi|Choshi City]] of [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]]. It has long been venerated for practices such as {{ill|Directional Deity|de|Richtgott|ja|方位神|1=Directional deity|lt=Eight-Directional Protection|lt_ja=八方除け|lt_de=Achtseitiger Schutz|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Directional Deity|13=User:Immanuelle/Directional deity|qq=}}. Its principal deities are [[:en:Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko Ōkami]], [[:en:Ame-no-Uzume|Ame-no-Uzume]], and [[:en:Kukurihime|Kukuri-hime-no-Mikoto]].
== History == According to shrine tradition, it was founded on November 25 in the 25th year of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]], and during the reign of [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]], it became a branch shrine of [[:en:Ikuta Shrine|Ikuta Shrine]]. In the 2nd year of [[:en:Daidō|Daido]] ([[:en:807|807 AD]]), a shrine building was constructed, and during the [[:en:Kōhei|Kōhei Era]] ([[:en:1058|1058 AD]] – [[:en:1065|1065 AD]]), [[:en:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] donated [[:en:Kanda|Kanda]]. Since the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], the warrior families ({{ill|Buke (Japan)|de|Kriegerfamilien|ja|武家|lt=Buke|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buke (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=buke|WD=Q2927809}}) have shown strong devotion, and it is said that [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] also made donations, with {{ill|Ashikaga Harushi|de|Ashikaga Harushi|ja|足利晴氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashikaga Harushi|1=Ashikaga Haruuji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11256588}} having offered a golden seal that still exists today.
In the 9th year of the [[:en:Eiroku|Eiroku Era]] ([[:en:1566|1566 AD]]), the shrine building was burned down in a battle between the {{ill|Kaijō Clan|de|Kaijō-Clan|ja|海上氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaijō Clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the {{ill|Awa Masaki Clan|de|Awa Masaki-Clan|ja|安房正木氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awa Masaki Clan|1=Awa Masaki Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450720}}. Although it was rebuilt during the [[:en:Genki (era)|Genki Era]], it was again burned down in the 1st year of the [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] ([[:en:1573|1573 AD]]) and rebuilt in the following 2nd year of Tenshō ([[:en:1574|1574 AD]]). The current main hall was reconstructed in the 8th year of the [[:en:Enpō|Enpō Era]] ([[:en:1680|1680 AD]]). Additionally, in the 19th year of Tenshō ([[:en:1591|1591 AD]]), [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] donated 30 stones of {{ill|Red Seal Land|de|Rotsiegel-Land|ja|朱印地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Red Seal Land|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 朱印地・黒印地|1=shuinchi and kokuinchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11521520}}.
Every 60 years, in the {{ill|Kōshin Year|de|Kōshin-Jahr|ja|庚申|1=Kōshin|lt=Yang Metal Monkey|2=ja|3=庚申|4=zh|5=庚申|6=ko|7=경신|lt_ko=경신|8=fr|9=Singe de métal|10=de|11=Metall-Affe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin Year|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, the Shikinen Taisai Kamiyuki-sai is held.
== Cultural Properties == * One plaque from the annex of the Saruta Shrine main hall ({{ill|Chiba Prefecture-designated Tangible Cultural Property|de|Chiba-Präfektur-designierte greifbare Kulturimmobilie|ja|千葉県指定文化財一覧#建造物|lt=Chiba Prefecture-designated Tangible Cultural Property|lt_ja=千葉県指定有形文化財|lt_de=Chiba-Präfektur-designierte greifbare Kulturimmobilie|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiba Prefecture-designated Tangible Cultural Property|1=Chiba Prefecture-designated Tangible Cultural Property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406175}}) * The forest of Saruta Shrine ({{ill|Chiba Prefecture-designated Natural Monument|de|Chiba-Präfektur-designiertes Naturdenkmal|ja|千葉県指定文化財一覧#天然記念物|lt=Chiba Prefecture-designated Natural Monument|lt_ja=千葉県指定天然記念物|lt_de=Chiba-Präfektur-designiertes Naturdenkmal|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiba Prefecture-designated Natural Monument|1=Chiba Prefecture-designated Natural Monument|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406175}})
== Access == * 5-minute walk from [[:en:Saruda Station|Saruta Station]] on the [[:en:Sōbu Main Line|Sōbu Main Line]]. * From either [[:en:Japan National Route 126|National Route 126]] at Asahi City's Ato or [[:en:Japan National Route 356|National Route 356]] at Choshi City's Nojiri, via the {{ill|Choshi-Asahi Line (Chiba Prefectural Road 71)|de|Choshi-Asahi-Linie (Chiba-Prefekturstraße 71)|ja|千葉県道71号銚子旭線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Choshi-Asahi Line (Chiba Prefectural Road 71)|1=Chiba Prefectural Road Route 71|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406653}}.
== Other == * There is a bridge called Sakushinbashi on the approach road that spans the JR [[:en:Sōbu Main Line|Sōbu Main Line]]. It was constructed by the railway company when the {{ill|Sōbu Railway (first generation)|de|Sōbu-Eisenbahn (Erste Generation)|ja|総武鉄道 (初代)|lt=Sōbu Railway|lt_ja=総武鉄道|lt_de=Sōbu Eisenbahn|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōbu Railway (first generation)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 総武本線|1=Sōbu Main Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1191364}} was extended to [[:en:Chōshi Station|Choshi Station]] in [[:en:1897|1897 AD]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 30), which resulted in the approach road being divided. * Due to the remnants of [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]], the shrine office is closed on Butsumetsu ({{ill|Butsumetsu (Buddhist calendar)|de|Butsumetsu|ja|六曜#仏滅|lt=Butsumetsu|lt_ja=仏滅|lt_de=Butsumetsu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Butsumetsu (Buddhist calendar)|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}) day.
== References == {{No footnotes|date=2020年2月|section=1}} * 『National Shrine Directory, Volume 1』, National Shrine Directory Publishing Society History Center, Showa 52. * 『Kadokawa Great Dictionary of Japanese Place Names, Volume 12: Chiba Prefecture』, Kadokawa Great Dictionary of Japanese Place Names Editorial Committee, Showa 59.
== External Links == * [http://www.choshikanko.com/extra/saruta/ Choshi City Saruta Shrine]

'''Saruta Shrine''' (猿田神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shrine]] located in Saruta-machi, [[:en:Chōshi|Choshi City]] of [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]]. It has long been venerated for practices such as {{ill|Directional Deity|de|Richtgott|ja|方位神|1=Directional deity|lt=Eight-Directional Protection|lt_ja=八方除け|lt_de=Achtseitiger Schutz|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Directional Deity|13=User:Immanuelle/Directional deity|qq=}}. Its principal deities are [[:en:Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko Ōkami]], [[:en:Ame-no-Uzume|Ame-no-Uzume]], and [[:en:Kukurihime|Kukuri-hime-no-Mikoto]].
== History == According to shrine tradition, it was founded on November 25 in the 25th year of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]], and during the reign of [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]], it became a branch shrine of [[:en:Ikuta Shrine|Ikuta Shrine]]. In the 2nd year of [[:en:Daidō|Daido]] ([[:en:807|807 AD]]), a shrine building was constructed, and during the [[:en:Kōhei|Kōhei Era]] ([[:en:1058|1058 AD]] – [[:en:1065|1065 AD]]), [[:en:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] donated [[:en:Kanda|Kanda]]. Since the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], the warrior families ({{ill|Buke (Japan)|de|Kriegerfamilien|ja|武家|lt=Buke|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buke (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=buke|WD=Q2927809}}) have shown strong devotion, and it is said that [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] also made donations, with {{ill|Ashikaga Harushi|de|Ashikaga Harushi|ja|足利晴氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashikaga Harushi|1=Ashikaga Haruuji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11256588}} having offered a golden seal that still exists today.
In the 9th year of the [[:en:Eiroku|Eiroku Era]] ([[:en:1566|1566 AD]]), the shrine building was burned down in a battle between the {{ill|Kaijō Clan|de|Kaijō-Clan|ja|海上氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaijō Clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the {{ill|Awa Masaki Clan|de|Awa Masaki-Clan|ja|安房正木氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awa Masaki Clan|1=Awa Masaki Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450720}}. Although it was rebuilt during the [[:en:Genki (era)|Genki Era]], it was again burned down in the 1st year of the [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] ([[:en:1573|1573 AD]]) and rebuilt in the following 2nd year of Tenshō ([[:en:1574|1574 AD]]). The current main hall was reconstructed in the 8th year of the [[:en:Enpō|Enpō Era]] ([[:en:1680|1680 AD]]). Additionally, in the 19th year of Tenshō ([[:en:1591|1591 AD]]), [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] donated 30 stones of {{ill|Red Seal Land|de|Rotsiegel-Land|ja|朱印地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Red Seal Land|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 朱印地・黒印地|1=shuinchi and kokuinchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11521520}}.
Every 60 years, in the {{ill|Kōshin Year|de|Kōshin-Jahr|ja|庚申|1=Kōshin|lt=Yang Metal Monkey|2=ja|3=庚申|4=zh|5=庚申|6=ko|7=경신|lt_ko=경신|8=fr|9=Singe de métal|10=de|11=Metall-Affe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin Year|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, the Shikinen Taisai Kamiyuki-sai is held.
== Cultural Properties == * One plaque from the annex of the Saruta Shrine main hall ({{ill|Chiba Prefecture-designated Tangible Cultural Property|de|Chiba-Präfektur-designierte greifbare Kulturimmobilie|ja|千葉県指定文化財一覧#建造物|lt=Chiba Prefecture-designated Tangible Cultural Property|lt_ja=千葉県指定有形文化財|lt_de=Chiba-Präfektur-designierte greifbare Kulturimmobilie|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiba Prefecture-designated Tangible Cultural Property|1=Chiba Prefecture-designated Tangible Cultural Property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406175}}) * The forest of Saruta Shrine ({{ill|Chiba Prefecture-designated Natural Monument|de|Chiba-Präfektur-designiertes Naturdenkmal|ja|千葉県指定文化財一覧#天然記念物|lt=Chiba Prefecture-designated Natural Monument|lt_ja=千葉県指定天然記念物|lt_de=Chiba-Präfektur-designiertes Naturdenkmal|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiba Prefecture-designated Natural Monument|1=Chiba Prefecture-designated Natural Monument|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406175}})
== Access == * 5-minute walk from [[:en:Saruda Station|Saruta Station]] on the [[:en:Sōbu Main Line|Sōbu Main Line]]. * From either [[:en:Japan National Route 126|National Route 126]] at Asahi City's Ato or [[:en:Japan National Route 356|National Route 356]] at Choshi City's Nojiri, via the {{ill|Choshi-Asahi Line (Chiba Prefectural Road 71)|de|Choshi-Asahi-Linie (Chiba-Prefekturstraße 71)|ja|千葉県道71号銚子旭線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Choshi-Asahi Line (Chiba Prefectural Road 71)|1=Chiba Prefectural Road Route 71|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406653}}.
== Other == * There is a bridge called Sakushinbashi on the approach road that spans the JR [[:en:Sōbu Main Line|Sōbu Main Line]]. It was constructed by the railway company when the {{ill|Sōbu Railway (first generation)|de|Sōbu-Eisenbahn (Erste Generation)|ja|総武鉄道 (初代)|lt=Sōbu Railway|lt_ja=総武鉄道|lt_de=Sōbu Eisenbahn|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōbu Railway (first generation)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 総武本線|1=Sōbu Main Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1191364}} was extended to [[:en:Chōshi Station|Choshi Station]] in [[:en:1897|1897 AD]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 30), which resulted in the approach road being divided. * Due to the remnants of [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]], the shrine office is closed on Butsumetsu ({{ill|Butsumetsu (Buddhist calendar)|de|Butsumetsu|ja|六曜#仏滅|lt=Butsumetsu|lt_ja=仏滅|lt_de=Butsumetsu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Butsumetsu (Buddhist calendar)|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}) day.
== References == {{No footnotes|date=2020年2月|section=1}} * 『National Shrine Directory, Volume 1』, National Shrine Directory Publishing Society History Center, Showa 52. * 『Kadokawa Great Dictionary of Japanese Place Names, Volume 12: Chiba Prefecture』, Kadokawa Great Dictionary of Japanese Place Names Editorial Committee, Showa 59.
== External Links == * [http://www.choshikanko.com/extra/saruta/ Choshi City Saruta Shrine]

== Access == * 5-minute walk from [[:en:Saruda Station|Saruta Station]] on the [[:en:Sōbu Main Line|Sōbu Main Line]]. * From either [[:en:Japan National Route 126|National Route 126]] at Asahi City's Ato or [[:en:Japan National Route 356|National Route 356]] at Choshi City's Nojiri, via the {{ill|Choshi-Asahi Line (Chiba Prefectural Road 71)|de|Choshi-Asahi-Linie (Chiba-Prefekturstraße 71)|ja|千葉県道71号銚子旭線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Choshi-Asahi Line (Chiba Prefectural Road 71)|1=Chiba Prefectural Road Route 71|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406653}}.
== Other == * There is a bridge called Sakushinbashi on the approach road that spans the JR [[:en:Sōbu Main Line|Sōbu Main Line]]. It was constructed by the railway company when the {{ill|Sōbu Railway (first generation)|de|Sōbu-Eisenbahn (Erste Generation)|ja|総武鉄道 (初代)|lt=Sōbu Railway|lt_ja=総武鉄道|lt_de=Sōbu Eisenbahn|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōbu Railway (first generation)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 総武本線|1=Sōbu Main Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1191364}} was extended to [[:en:Chōshi Station|Choshi Station]] in [[:en:1897|1897 AD]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 30), which resulted in the approach road being divided. * Due to the remnants of [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]], the shrine office is closed on Butsumetsu ({{ill|Butsumetsu (Buddhist calendar)|de|Butsumetsu|ja|六曜#仏滅|lt=Butsumetsu|lt_ja=仏滅|lt_de=Butsumetsu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Butsumetsu (Buddhist calendar)|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}) day.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:猿田神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11572147]] Wikidata name: Saruda-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:07, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name= Saruta Shrine |image= File:Saruda-jinjya-shrine,choshi-city,japan.JPG |Location= [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]] [[:en:Chōshi|Choshi City]] Saruta-machi 1677 |Coordinates= {{WikiCoordinatesTwoTierDMS|35|44|50|N|140|44|0|E|scale:25000}} |Enshrined Deities= [[:en:Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko Ōkami]]・[[:en:Ame-no-Uzume|Ame-no-Uzume]]・[[:en:Kukurihime|Kukuri-hime-no-Mikoto]] |Shrine Rank= Unranked Shrine |Established= 25th year of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]] |Main Hall= Nagare-zukuri |Alternate Name= |Annual Festival= November 15 |Sacred Rites= Shikinen Taisai Kamiyuki-sai |prefecture= {{ill|Chōshi|WD=Q694317}} |coordinates= {{Coord|35.747222|N|140.733333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity= {{ill|Ama-no-Uzume|WD=Q462803}}; {{ill|Kukurihime|WD=Q11619891}}; {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|WD=Q3090037}} }}
'''Saruta Shrine''' (猿田神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shrine]] located in Saruta-machi, [[:en:Chōshi|Choshi City]] of [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]]. It has long been venerated for practices such as {{ill|Directional Deity|de|Richtgott|ja|方位神|1=Directional deity|lt=Eight-Directional Protection|lt_ja=八方除け|lt_de=Achtseitiger Schutz|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Directional Deity|13=User:Immanuelle/Directional deity|qq=}}. Its principal deities are [[:en:Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko Ōkami]], [[:en:Ame-no-Uzume|Ame-no-Uzume]], and [[:en:Kukurihime|Kukuri-hime-no-Mikoto]].
== History == According to shrine tradition, it was founded on November 25 in the 25th year of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]], and during the reign of [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]], it became a branch shrine of [[:en:Ikuta Shrine|Ikuta Shrine]]. In the 2nd year of [[:en:Daidō|Daido]] ([[:en:807|807 AD]]), a shrine building was constructed, and during the [[:en:Kōhei|Kōhei Era]] ([[:en:1058|1058 AD]] – [[:en:1065|1065 AD]]), [[:en:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] donated [[:en:Kanda|Kanda]]. Since the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], the warrior families ({{ill|Buke (Japan)|de|Kriegerfamilien|ja|武家|lt=Buke|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buke (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=buke|WD=Q2927809}}) have shown strong devotion, and it is said that [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] also made donations, with {{ill|Ashikaga Harushi|de|Ashikaga Harushi|ja|足利晴氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashikaga Harushi|1=Ashikaga Haruuji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11256588}} having offered a golden seal that still exists today.
In the 9th year of the [[:en:Eiroku|Eiroku Era]] ([[:en:1566|1566 AD]]), the shrine building was burned down in a battle between the {{ill|Kaijō Clan|de|Kaijō-Clan|ja|海上氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaijō Clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the {{ill|Awa Masaki Clan|de|Awa Masaki-Clan|ja|安房正木氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awa Masaki Clan|1=Awa Masaki Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450720}}. Although it was rebuilt during the [[:en:Genki (era)|Genki Era]], it was again burned down in the 1st year of the [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] ([[:en:1573|1573 AD]]) and rebuilt in the following 2nd year of Tenshō ([[:en:1574|1574 AD]]). The current main hall was reconstructed in the 8th year of the [[:en:Enpō|Enpō Era]] ([[:en:1680|1680 AD]]). Additionally, in the 19th year of Tenshō ([[:en:1591|1591 AD]]), [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] donated 30 stones of {{ill|Red Seal Land|de|Rotsiegel-Land|ja|朱印地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Red Seal Land|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 朱印地・黒印地|1=shuinchi and kokuinchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11521520}}.
Every 60 years, in the {{ill|Kōshin Year|de|Kōshin-Jahr|ja|庚申|1=Kōshin|lt=Yang Metal Monkey|2=ja|3=庚申|4=zh|5=庚申|6=ko|7=경신|lt_ko=경신|8=fr|9=Singe de métal|10=de|11=Metall-Affe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin Year|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, the Shikinen Taisai Kamiyuki-sai is held.
== Cultural Properties == * One plaque from the annex of the Saruta Shrine main hall ({{ill|Chiba Prefecture-designated Tangible Cultural Property|de|Chiba-Präfektur-designierte greifbare Kulturimmobilie|ja|千葉県指定文化財一覧#建造物|lt=Chiba Prefecture-designated Tangible Cultural Property|lt_ja=千葉県指定有形文化財|lt_de=Chiba-Präfektur-designierte greifbare Kulturimmobilie|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiba Prefecture-designated Tangible Cultural Property|1=Chiba Prefecture-designated Tangible Cultural Property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406175}}) * The forest of Saruta Shrine ({{ill|Chiba Prefecture-designated Natural Monument|de|Chiba-Präfektur-designiertes Naturdenkmal|ja|千葉県指定文化財一覧#天然記念物|lt=Chiba Prefecture-designated Natural Monument|lt_ja=千葉県指定天然記念物|lt_de=Chiba-Präfektur-designiertes Naturdenkmal|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiba Prefecture-designated Natural Monument|1=Chiba Prefecture-designated Natural Monument|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406175}})
== Access == * 5-minute walk from [[:en:Saruda Station|Saruta Station]] on the [[:en:Sōbu Main Line|Sōbu Main Line]]. * From either [[:en:Japan National Route 126|National Route 126]] at Asahi City's Ato or [[:en:Japan National Route 356|National Route 356]] at Choshi City's Nojiri, via the {{ill|Choshi-Asahi Line (Chiba Prefectural Road 71)|de|Choshi-Asahi-Linie (Chiba-Prefekturstraße 71)|ja|千葉県道71号銚子旭線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Choshi-Asahi Line (Chiba Prefectural Road 71)|1=Chiba Prefectural Road Route 71|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406653}}.
== Other == * There is a bridge called Sakushinbashi on the approach road that spans the JR [[:en:Sōbu Main Line|Sōbu Main Line]]. It was constructed by the railway company when the {{ill|Sōbu Railway (first generation)|de|Sōbu-Eisenbahn (Erste Generation)|ja|総武鉄道 (初代)|lt=Sōbu Railway|lt_ja=総武鉄道|lt_de=Sōbu Eisenbahn|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōbu Railway (first generation)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 総武本線|1=Sōbu Main Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1191364}} was extended to [[:en:Chōshi Station|Choshi Station]] in [[:en:1897|1897 AD]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 30), which resulted in the approach road being divided. * Due to the remnants of [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]], the shrine office is closed on Butsumetsu ({{ill|Butsumetsu (Buddhist calendar)|de|Butsumetsu|ja|六曜#仏滅|lt=Butsumetsu|lt_ja=仏滅|lt_de=Butsumetsu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Butsumetsu (Buddhist calendar)|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}) day.
== References == {{No footnotes|date=2020年2月|section=1}} * 『National Shrine Directory, Volume 1』, National Shrine Directory Publishing Society History Center, Showa 52. * 『Kadokawa Great Dictionary of Japanese Place Names, Volume 12: Chiba Prefecture』, Kadokawa Great Dictionary of Japanese Place Names Editorial Committee, Showa 59.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:猿田神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:猿田神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11572147]] Wikidata name: Saruda-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:07, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name= Saruta Shrine |image= File:Saruda-jinjya-shrine,choshi-city,japan.JPG |Location= [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]] [[:en:Chōshi|Choshi City]] Saruta-machi 1677 |Coordinates= {{WikiCoordinatesTwoTierDMS|35|44|50|N|140|44|0|E|scale:25000}} |Enshrined Deities= [[:en:Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko Ōkami]]・[[:en:Ame-no-Uzume|Ame-no-Uzume]]・[[:en:Kukurihime|Kukuri-hime-no-Mikoto]] |Shrine Rank= Unranked Shrine |Established= 25th year of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]] |Main Hall= Nagare-zukuri |Alternate Name= |Annual Festival= November 15 |Sacred Rites= Shikinen Taisai Kamiyuki-sai |prefecture= {{ill|Chōshi|WD=Q694317}} |coordinates= {{Coord|35.747222|N|140.733333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity= {{ill|Ama-no-Uzume|WD=Q462803}}; {{ill|Kukurihime|WD=Q11619891}}; {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|WD=Q3090037}} }}
'''Saruta Shrine''' (猿田神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shrine]] located in Saruta-machi, [[:en:Chōshi|Choshi City]] of [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]]. It has long been venerated for practices such as {{ill|Directional Deity|de|Richtgott|ja|方位神|1=Directional deity|lt=Eight-Directional Protection|lt_ja=八方除け|lt_de=Achtseitiger Schutz|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Directional Deity|13=User:Immanuelle/Directional deity|qq=}}. Its principal deities are [[:en:Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko Ōkami]], [[:en:Ame-no-Uzume|Ame-no-Uzume]], and [[:en:Kukurihime|Kukuri-hime-no-Mikoto]].
== History == According to shrine tradition, it was founded on November 25 in the 25th year of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]], and during the reign of [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]], it became a branch shrine of [[:en:Ikuta Shrine|Ikuta Shrine]]. In the 2nd year of [[:en:Daidō|Daido]] ([[:en:807|807 AD]]), a shrine building was constructed, and during the [[:en:Kōhei|Kōhei Era]] ([[:en:1058|1058 AD]] – [[:en:1065|1065 AD]]), [[:en:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] donated [[:en:Kanda|Kanda]]. Since the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], the warrior families ({{ill|Buke (Japan)|de|Kriegerfamilien|ja|武家|lt=Buke|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buke (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=buke|WD=Q2927809}}) have shown strong devotion, and it is said that [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] also made donations, with {{ill|Ashikaga Harushi|de|Ashikaga Harushi|ja|足利晴氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashikaga Harushi|1=Ashikaga Haruuji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11256588}} having offered a golden seal that still exists today.
In the 9th year of the [[:en:Eiroku|Eiroku Era]] ([[:en:1566|1566 AD]]), the shrine building was burned down in a battle between the {{ill|Kaijō Clan|de|Kaijō-Clan|ja|海上氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaijō Clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the {{ill|Awa Masaki Clan|de|Awa Masaki-Clan|ja|安房正木氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awa Masaki Clan|1=Awa Masaki Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450720}}. Although it was rebuilt during the [[:en:Genki (era)|Genki Era]], it was again burned down in the 1st year of the [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] ([[:en:1573|1573 AD]]) and rebuilt in the following 2nd year of Tenshō ([[:en:1574|1574 AD]]). The current main hall was reconstructed in the 8th year of the [[:en:Enpō|Enpō Era]] ([[:en:1680|1680 AD]]). Additionally, in the 19th year of Tenshō ([[:en:1591|1591 AD]]), [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] donated 30 stones of {{ill|Red Seal Land|de|Rotsiegel-Land|ja|朱印地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Red Seal Land|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 朱印地・黒印地|1=shuinchi and kokuinchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11521520}}.
Every 60 years, in the {{ill|Kōshin Year|de|Kōshin-Jahr|ja|庚申|1=Kōshin|lt=Yang Metal Monkey|2=ja|3=庚申|4=zh|5=庚申|6=ko|7=경신|lt_ko=경신|8=fr|9=Singe de métal|10=de|11=Metall-Affe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin Year|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, the Shikinen Taisai Kamiyuki-sai is held.
== Cultural Properties == * One plaque from the annex of the Saruta Shrine main hall ({{ill|Chiba Prefecture-designated Tangible Cultural Property|de|Chiba-Präfektur-designierte greifbare Kulturimmobilie|ja|千葉県指定文化財一覧#建造物|lt=Chiba Prefecture-designated Tangible Cultural Property|lt_ja=千葉県指定有形文化財|lt_de=Chiba-Präfektur-designierte greifbare Kulturimmobilie|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiba Prefecture-designated Tangible Cultural Property|1=Chiba Prefecture-designated Tangible Cultural Property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406175}}) * The forest of Saruta Shrine ({{ill|Chiba Prefecture-designated Natural Monument|de|Chiba-Präfektur-designiertes Naturdenkmal|ja|千葉県指定文化財一覧#天然記念物|lt=Chiba Prefecture-designated Natural Monument|lt_ja=千葉県指定天然記念物|lt_de=Chiba-Präfektur-designiertes Naturdenkmal|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiba Prefecture-designated Natural Monument|1=Chiba Prefecture-designated Natural Monument|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406175}})
== Access == * 5-minute walk from [[:en:Saruda Station|Saruta Station]] on the [[:en:Sōbu Main Line|Sōbu Main Line]]. * From either [[:en:Japan National Route 126|National Route 126]] at Asahi City's Ato or [[:en:Japan National Route 356|National Route 356]] at Choshi City's Nojiri, via the {{ill|Choshi-Asahi Line (Chiba Prefectural Road 71)|de|Choshi-Asahi-Linie (Chiba-Prefekturstraße 71)|ja|千葉県道71号銚子旭線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Choshi-Asahi Line (Chiba Prefectural Road 71)|1=Chiba Prefectural Road Route 71|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406653}}.
== Other == * There is a bridge called Sakushinbashi on the approach road that spans the JR [[:en:Sōbu Main Line|Sōbu Main Line]]. It was constructed by the railway company when the {{ill|Sōbu Railway (first generation)|de|Sōbu-Eisenbahn (Erste Generation)|ja|総武鉄道 (初代)|lt=Sōbu Railway|lt_ja=総武鉄道|lt_de=Sōbu Eisenbahn|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōbu Railway (first generation)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 総武本線|1=Sōbu Main Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1191364}} was extended to [[:en:Chōshi Station|Choshi Station]] in [[:en:1897|1897 AD]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 30), which resulted in the approach road being divided. * Due to the remnants of [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]], the shrine office is closed on Butsumetsu ({{ill|Butsumetsu (Buddhist calendar)|de|Butsumetsu|ja|六曜#仏滅|lt=Butsumetsu|lt_ja=仏滅|lt_de=Butsumetsu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Butsumetsu (Buddhist calendar)|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}) day.
== References == {{No footnotes|date=2020年2月|section=1}} * 『National Shrine Directory, Volume 1』, National Shrine Directory Publishing Society History Center, Showa 52. * 『Kadokawa Great Dictionary of Japanese Place Names, Volume 12: Chiba Prefecture』, Kadokawa Great Dictionary of Japanese Place Names Editorial Committee, Showa 59.
== External Links == * [http://www.choshikanko.com/extra/saruta/ Choshi City Saruta Shrine]

== Cultural Properties == * One plaque from the annex of the Saruta Shrine main hall ({{ill|Chiba Prefecture-designated Tangible Cultural Property|de|Chiba-Präfektur-designierte greifbare Kulturimmobilie|ja|千葉県指定文化財一覧#建造物|lt=Chiba Prefecture-designated Tangible Cultural Property|lt_ja=千葉県指定有形文化財|lt_de=Chiba-Präfektur-designierte greifbare Kulturimmobilie|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiba Prefecture-designated Tangible Cultural Property|1=Chiba Prefecture-designated Tangible Cultural Property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406175}}) * The forest of Saruta Shrine ({{ill|Chiba Prefecture-designated Natural Monument|de|Chiba-Präfektur-designiertes Naturdenkmal|ja|千葉県指定文化財一覧#天然記念物|lt=Chiba Prefecture-designated Natural Monument|lt_ja=千葉県指定天然記念物|lt_de=Chiba-Präfektur-designiertes Naturdenkmal|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiba Prefecture-designated Natural Monument|1=Chiba Prefecture-designated Natural Monument|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406175}})
== Access == * 5-minute walk from [[:en:Saruda Station|Saruta Station]] on the [[:en:Sōbu Main Line|Sōbu Main Line]]. * From either [[:en:Japan National Route 126|National Route 126]] at Asahi City's Ato or [[:en:Japan National Route 356|National Route 356]] at Choshi City's Nojiri, via the {{ill|Choshi-Asahi Line (Chiba Prefectural Road 71)|de|Choshi-Asahi-Linie (Chiba-Prefekturstraße 71)|ja|千葉県道71号銚子旭線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Choshi-Asahi Line (Chiba Prefectural Road 71)|1=Chiba Prefectural Road Route 71|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406653}}.
== Other == * There is a bridge called Sakushinbashi on the approach road that spans the JR [[:en:Sōbu Main Line|Sōbu Main Line]]. It was constructed by the railway company when the {{ill|Sōbu Railway (first generation)|de|Sōbu-Eisenbahn (Erste Generation)|ja|総武鉄道 (初代)|lt=Sōbu Railway|lt_ja=総武鉄道|lt_de=Sōbu Eisenbahn|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōbu Railway (first generation)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 総武本線|1=Sōbu Main Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1191364}} was extended to [[:en:Chōshi Station|Choshi Station]] in [[:en:1897|1897 AD]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 30), which resulted in the approach road being divided. * Due to the remnants of [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]], the shrine office is closed on Butsumetsu ({{ill|Butsumetsu (Buddhist calendar)|de|Butsumetsu|ja|六曜#仏滅|lt=Butsumetsu|lt_ja=仏滅|lt_de=Butsumetsu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Butsumetsu (Buddhist calendar)|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}) day.
== References == {{No footnotes|date=2020年2月|section=1}} * 『National Shrine Directory, Volume 1』, National Shrine Directory Publishing Society History Center, Showa 52. * 『Kadokawa Great Dictionary of Japanese Place Names, Volume 12: Chiba Prefecture』, Kadokawa Great Dictionary of Japanese Place Names Editorial Committee, Showa 59.
== External Links == * [http://www.choshikanko.com/extra/saruta/ Choshi City Saruta Shrine]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:猿田神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:猿田神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:猿田神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11572147]] Wikidata name: Saruda-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:07, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name= Saruta Shrine |image= File:Saruda-jinjya-shrine,choshi-city,japan.JPG |Location= [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]] [[:en:Chōshi|Choshi City]] Saruta-machi 1677 |Coordinates= {{WikiCoordinatesTwoTierDMS|35|44|50|N|140|44|0|E|scale:25000}} |Enshrined Deities= [[:en:Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko Ōkami]]・[[:en:Ame-no-Uzume|Ame-no-Uzume]]・[[:en:Kukurihime|Kukuri-hime-no-Mikoto]] |Shrine Rank= Unranked Shrine |Established= 25th year of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]] |Main Hall= Nagare-zukuri |Alternate Name= |Annual Festival= November 15 |Sacred Rites= Shikinen Taisai Kamiyuki-sai |prefecture= {{ill|Chōshi|WD=Q694317}} |coordinates= {{Coord|35.747222|N|140.733333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity= {{ill|Ama-no-Uzume|WD=Q462803}}; {{ill|Kukurihime|WD=Q11619891}}; {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|WD=Q3090037}} }}
'''Saruta Shrine''' (猿田神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shrine]] located in Saruta-machi, [[:en:Chōshi|Choshi City]] of [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]]. It has long been venerated for practices such as {{ill|Directional Deity|de|Richtgott|ja|方位神|1=Directional deity|lt=Eight-Directional Protection|lt_ja=八方除け|lt_de=Achtseitiger Schutz|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Directional Deity|13=User:Immanuelle/Directional deity|qq=}}. Its principal deities are [[:en:Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko Ōkami]], [[:en:Ame-no-Uzume|Ame-no-Uzume]], and [[:en:Kukurihime|Kukuri-hime-no-Mikoto]].
== History == According to shrine tradition, it was founded on November 25 in the 25th year of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]], and during the reign of [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]], it became a branch shrine of [[:en:Ikuta Shrine|Ikuta Shrine]]. In the 2nd year of [[:en:Daidō|Daido]] ([[:en:807|807 AD]]), a shrine building was constructed, and during the [[:en:Kōhei|Kōhei Era]] ([[:en:1058|1058 AD]] – [[:en:1065|1065 AD]]), [[:en:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] donated [[:en:Kanda|Kanda]]. Since the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], the warrior families ({{ill|Buke (Japan)|de|Kriegerfamilien|ja|武家|lt=Buke|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buke (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=buke|WD=Q2927809}}) have shown strong devotion, and it is said that [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] also made donations, with {{ill|Ashikaga Harushi|de|Ashikaga Harushi|ja|足利晴氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashikaga Harushi|1=Ashikaga Haruuji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11256588}} having offered a golden seal that still exists today.
In the 9th year of the [[:en:Eiroku|Eiroku Era]] ([[:en:1566|1566 AD]]), the shrine building was burned down in a battle between the {{ill|Kaijō Clan|de|Kaijō-Clan|ja|海上氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaijō Clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the {{ill|Awa Masaki Clan|de|Awa Masaki-Clan|ja|安房正木氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awa Masaki Clan|1=Awa Masaki Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450720}}. Although it was rebuilt during the [[:en:Genki (era)|Genki Era]], it was again burned down in the 1st year of the [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] ([[:en:1573|1573 AD]]) and rebuilt in the following 2nd year of Tenshō ([[:en:1574|1574 AD]]). The current main hall was reconstructed in the 8th year of the [[:en:Enpō|Enpō Era]] ([[:en:1680|1680 AD]]). Additionally, in the 19th year of Tenshō ([[:en:1591|1591 AD]]), [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] donated 30 stones of {{ill|Red Seal Land|de|Rotsiegel-Land|ja|朱印地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Red Seal Land|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 朱印地・黒印地|1=shuinchi and kokuinchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11521520}}.
Every 60 years, in the {{ill|Kōshin Year|de|Kōshin-Jahr|ja|庚申|1=Kōshin|lt=Yang Metal Monkey|2=ja|3=庚申|4=zh|5=庚申|6=ko|7=경신|lt_ko=경신|8=fr|9=Singe de métal|10=de|11=Metall-Affe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin Year|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, the Shikinen Taisai Kamiyuki-sai is held.
== Cultural Properties == * One plaque from the annex of the Saruta Shrine main hall ({{ill|Chiba Prefecture-designated Tangible Cultural Property|de|Chiba-Präfektur-designierte greifbare Kulturimmobilie|ja|千葉県指定文化財一覧#建造物|lt=Chiba Prefecture-designated Tangible Cultural Property|lt_ja=千葉県指定有形文化財|lt_de=Chiba-Präfektur-designierte greifbare Kulturimmobilie|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiba Prefecture-designated Tangible Cultural Property|1=Chiba Prefecture-designated Tangible Cultural Property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406175}}) * The forest of Saruta Shrine ({{ill|Chiba Prefecture-designated Natural Monument|de|Chiba-Präfektur-designiertes Naturdenkmal|ja|千葉県指定文化財一覧#天然記念物|lt=Chiba Prefecture-designated Natural Monument|lt_ja=千葉県指定天然記念物|lt_de=Chiba-Präfektur-designiertes Naturdenkmal|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiba Prefecture-designated Natural Monument|1=Chiba Prefecture-designated Natural Monument|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406175}})
== Access == * 5-minute walk from [[:en:Saruda Station|Saruta Station]] on the [[:en:Sōbu Main Line|Sōbu Main Line]]. * From either [[:en:Japan National Route 126|National Route 126]] at Asahi City's Ato or [[:en:Japan National Route 356|National Route 356]] at Choshi City's Nojiri, via the {{ill|Choshi-Asahi Line (Chiba Prefectural Road 71)|de|Choshi-Asahi-Linie (Chiba-Prefekturstraße 71)|ja|千葉県道71号銚子旭線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Choshi-Asahi Line (Chiba Prefectural Road 71)|1=Chiba Prefectural Road Route 71|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406653}}.
== Other == * There is a bridge called Sakushinbashi on the approach road that spans the JR [[:en:Sōbu Main Line|Sōbu Main Line]]. It was constructed by the railway company when the {{ill|Sōbu Railway (first generation)|de|Sōbu-Eisenbahn (Erste Generation)|ja|総武鉄道 (初代)|lt=Sōbu Railway|lt_ja=総武鉄道|lt_de=Sōbu Eisenbahn|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōbu Railway (first generation)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 総武本線|1=Sōbu Main Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1191364}} was extended to [[:en:Chōshi Station|Choshi Station]] in [[:en:1897|1897 AD]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 30), which resulted in the approach road being divided. * Due to the remnants of [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]], the shrine office is closed on Butsumetsu ({{ill|Butsumetsu (Buddhist calendar)|de|Butsumetsu|ja|六曜#仏滅|lt=Butsumetsu|lt_ja=仏滅|lt_de=Butsumetsu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Butsumetsu (Buddhist calendar)|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}) day.
== References == {{No footnotes|date=2020年2月|section=1}} * 『National Shrine Directory, Volume 1』, National Shrine Directory Publishing Society History Center, Showa 52. * 『Kadokawa Great Dictionary of Japanese Place Names, Volume 12: Chiba Prefecture』, Kadokawa Great Dictionary of Japanese Place Names Editorial Committee, Showa 59.
