revision.comment: v25: Convert interwiki links [[:en:...]] to {{ill}} templates
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:猿田神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:猿田神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:猿田神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11572147]] Wikidata name: Saruda-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:07, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name= Saruta Shrine |image= File:Saruda-jinjya-shrine,choshi-city,japan.JPG |Location= {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture}} {{ill|Chōshi|en|Chōshi|WD=Q694317|lt=Choshi City}} Saruta-machi 1677 |Coordinates= {{WikiCoordinatesTwoTierDMS|35|44|50|N|140|44|0|E|scale:25000}} |Enshrined Deities= {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|en|Sarutahiko Ōkami|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami}}・{{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|en|Ame-no-Uzume|WD=Q462803|lt=Ame-no-Uzume}}・{{ill|Kukurihime|en|Kukurihime|WD=Q11619891|lt=Kukuri-hime-no-Mikoto}} |Shrine Rank= Unranked Shrine |Established= 25th year of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin}} |Main Hall= Nagare-zukuri |Alternate Name= |Annual Festival= November 15 |Sacred Rites= Shikinen Taisai Kamiyuki-sai |prefecture= {{ill|Chōshi|WD=Q694317}} |coordinates= {{Coord|35.747222|N|140.733333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity= {{ill|Ama-no-Uzume|WD=Q462803}}; {{ill|Kukurihime|WD=Q11619891}}; {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|WD=Q3090037}} }}
'''Saruta Shrine''' (猿田神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shrine}} located in Saruta-machi, {{ill|Chōshi|en|Chōshi|WD=Q694317|lt=Choshi City}} of {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture}}. It has long been venerated for practices such as {{ill|Directional Deity|de|Richtgott|ja|方位神|1=Directional deity|lt=Eight-Directional Protection|lt_ja=八方除け|lt_de=Achtseitiger Schutz|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Directional Deity|13=User:Immanuelle/Directional deity|qq=}}. Its principal deities are {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|en|Sarutahiko Ōkami|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|en|Ame-no-Uzume|WD=Q462803|lt=Ame-no-Uzume}}, and {{ill|Kukurihime|en|Kukurihime|WD=Q11619891|lt=Kukuri-hime-no-Mikoto}}.
== History == According to shrine tradition, it was founded on November 25 in the 25th year of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin}}, and during the reign of {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū}}, it became a branch shrine of {{ill|Ikuta Shrine|en|Ikuta Shrine|WD=Q710086|lt=Ikuta Shrine}}. In the 2nd year of {{ill|Daidō|en|Daidō|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daido}} ({{ill|807|en|807|WD=Q30016|lt=807 AD}}), a shrine building was constructed, and during the {{ill|Kōhei|en|Kōhei|WD=Q1193572|lt=Kōhei Era}} ({{ill|1058|en|1058|WD=Q19380|lt=1058 AD}} – {{ill|1065|en|1065|WD=Q19407|lt=1065 AD}}), {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|en|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|WD=Q3082851|lt=Minamoto no Yoriyoshi}} donated {{ill|Kanda|en|Kanda|WD=Q415424|lt=Kanda}}. Since the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period}}, the warrior families ({{ill|Buke (Japan)|de|Kriegerfamilien|ja|武家|lt=Buke|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buke (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=buke|WD=Q2927809}}) have shown strong devotion, and it is said that {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|en|Minamoto no Yoritomo|WD=Q242800|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo}} also made donations, with {{ill|Ashikaga Harushi|de|Ashikaga Harushi|ja|足利晴氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashikaga Harushi|1=Ashikaga Haruuji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11256588}} having offered a golden seal that still exists today.
In the 9th year of the {{ill|Eiroku|en|Eiroku|WD=Q1143341|lt=Eiroku Era}} ({{ill|1566|en|1566|WD=Q6638|lt=1566 AD}}), the shrine building was burned down in a battle between the {{ill|Kaijō Clan|de|Kaijō-Clan|ja|海上氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaijō Clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the {{ill|Awa Masaki Clan|de|Awa Masaki-Clan|ja|安房正木氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awa Masaki Clan|1=Awa Masaki Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450720}}. Although it was rebuilt during the {{ill|Genki (era)|en|Genki (era)|WD=Q1145064|lt=Genki Era}}, it was again burned down in the 1st year of the {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|WD=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era}} ({{ill|1573|en|1573|WD=Q6678|lt=1573 AD}}) and rebuilt in the following 2nd year of Tenshō ({{ill|1574|en|1574|WD=Q6680|lt=1574 AD}}). The current main hall was reconstructed in the 8th year of the {{ill|Enpō|en|Enpō|WD=Q1145735|lt=Enpō Era}} ({{ill|1680|en|1680|WD=Q7642|lt=1680 AD}}). Additionally, in the 19th year of Tenshō ({{ill|1591|en|1591|WD=Q6766|lt=1591 AD}}), {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|WD=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} donated 30 stones of {{ill|Red Seal Land|de|Rotsiegel-Land|ja|朱印地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Red Seal Land|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 朱印地・黒印地|1=shuinchi and kokuinchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11521520}}.
Every 60 years, in the {{ill|Kōshin Year|de|Kōshin-Jahr|ja|庚申|1=Kōshin|lt=Yang Metal Monkey|2=ja|3=庚申|4=zh|5=庚申|6=ko|7=경신|lt_ko=경신|8=fr|9=Singe de métal|10=de|11=Metall-Affe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin Year|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, the Shikinen Taisai Kamiyuki-sai is held.
== Cultural Properties == * One plaque from the annex of the Saruta Shrine main hall ({{ill|Chiba Prefecture-designated Tangible Cultural Property|de|Chiba-Präfektur-designierte greifbare Kulturimmobilie|ja|千葉県指定文化財一覧#建造物|lt=Chiba Prefecture-designated Tangible Cultural Property|lt_ja=千葉県指定有形文化財|lt_de=Chiba-Präfektur-designierte greifbare Kulturimmobilie|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiba Prefecture-designated Tangible Cultural Property|1=Chiba Prefecture-designated Tangible Cultural Property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406175}}) * The forest of Saruta Shrine ({{ill|Chiba Prefecture-designated Natural Monument|de|Chiba-Präfektur-designiertes Naturdenkmal|ja|千葉県指定文化財一覧#天然記念物|lt=Chiba Prefecture-designated Natural Monument|lt_ja=千葉県指定天然記念物|lt_de=Chiba-Präfektur-designiertes Naturdenkmal|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiba Prefecture-designated Natural Monument|1=Chiba Prefecture-designated Natural Monument|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406175}})
== Access == * 5-minute walk from {{ill|Saruda Station|en|Saruda Station|WD=Q5364199|lt=Saruta Station}} on the {{ill|Sōbu Main Line|en|Sōbu Main Line|WD=Q1191364|lt=Sōbu Main Line}}. * From either {{ill|Japan National Route 126|en|Japan National Route 126|WD=Q6156754|lt=National Route 126}} at Asahi City's Ato or {{ill|Japan National Route 356|en|Japan National Route 356|WD=Q6157325|lt=National Route 356}} at Choshi City's Nojiri, via the {{ill|Choshi-Asahi Line (Chiba Prefectural Road 71)|de|Choshi-Asahi-Linie (Chiba-Prefekturstraße 71)|ja|千葉県道71号銚子旭線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Choshi-Asahi Line (Chiba Prefectural Road 71)|1=Chiba Prefectural Road Route 71|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406653}}.
== Other == * There is a bridge called Sakushinbashi on the approach road that spans the JR {{ill|Sōbu Main Line|en|Sōbu Main Line|WD=Q1191364|lt=Sōbu Main Line}}. It was constructed by the railway company when the {{ill|Sōbu Railway (first generation)|de|Sōbu-Eisenbahn (Erste Generation)|ja|総武鉄道 (初代)|lt=Sōbu Railway|lt_ja=総武鉄道|lt_de=Sōbu Eisenbahn|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōbu Railway (first generation)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 総武本線|1=Sōbu Main Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1191364}} was extended to {{ill|Chōshi Station|en|Chōshi Station|WD=Q861084|lt=Choshi Station}} in {{ill|1897|en|1897|WD=Q7847|lt=1897 AD}} ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 30), which resulted in the approach road being divided. * Due to the remnants of {{ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|en|Shinbutsu-shūgō|WD=Q2279088|lt=Shinbutsu-shūgō}}, the shrine office is closed on Butsumetsu ({{ill|Butsumetsu (Buddhist calendar)|de|Butsumetsu|ja|六曜#仏滅|lt=Butsumetsu|lt_ja=仏滅|lt_de=Butsumetsu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Butsumetsu (Buddhist calendar)|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}) day.
== References == {{No footnotes|date=2020年2月|section=1}} * 『National Shrine Directory, Volume 1』, National Shrine Directory Publishing Society History Center, Showa 52. * 『Kadokawa Great Dictionary of Japanese Place Names, Volume 12: Chiba Prefecture』, Kadokawa Great Dictionary of Japanese Place Names Editorial Committee, Showa 59.
== External Links == * [http://www.choshikanko.com/extra/saruta/ Choshi City Saruta Shrine]
{{shinto-stub}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:猿田神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:猿田神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:猿田神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11572147]] Wikidata name: Saruda-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:07, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name= Saruta Shrine |image= File:Saruda-jinjya-shrine,choshi-city,japan.JPG |Location= {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture}} {{ill|Chōshi|en|Chōshi|WD=Q694317|lt=Choshi City}} Saruta-machi 1677 |Coordinates= {{WikiCoordinatesTwoTierDMS|35|44|50|N|140|44|0|E|scale:25000}} |Enshrined Deities= {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|en|Sarutahiko Ōkami|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami}}・{{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|en|Ame-no-Uzume|WD=Q462803|lt=Ame-no-Uzume}}・{{ill|Kukurihime|en|Kukurihime|WD=Q11619891|lt=Kukuri-hime-no-Mikoto}} |Shrine Rank= Unranked Shrine |Established= 25th year of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin}} |Main Hall= Nagare-zukuri |Alternate Name= |Annual Festival= November 15 |Sacred Rites= Shikinen Taisai Kamiyuki-sai |prefecture= {{ill|Chōshi|WD=Q694317}} |coordinates= {{Coord|35.747222|N|140.733333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity= {{ill|Ama-no-Uzume|WD=Q462803}}; {{ill|Kukurihime|WD=Q11619891}}; {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|WD=Q3090037}} }}
'''Saruta Shrine''' (猿田神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shrine}} located in Saruta-machi, {{ill|Chōshi|en|Chōshi|WD=Q694317|lt=Choshi City}} of {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture}}. It has long been venerated for practices such as {{ill|Directional Deity|de|Richtgott|ja|方位神|1=Directional deity|lt=Eight-Directional Protection|lt_ja=八方除け|lt_de=Achtseitiger Schutz|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Directional Deity|13=User:Immanuelle/Directional deity|qq=}}. Its principal deities are {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|en|Sarutahiko Ōkami|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|en|Ame-no-Uzume|WD=Q462803|lt=Ame-no-Uzume}}, and {{ill|Kukurihime|en|Kukurihime|WD=Q11619891|lt=Kukuri-hime-no-Mikoto}}.
== History == According to shrine tradition, it was founded on November 25 in the 25th year of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin}}, and during the reign of {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū}}, it became a branch shrine of {{ill|Ikuta Shrine|en|Ikuta Shrine|WD=Q710086|lt=Ikuta Shrine}}. In the 2nd year of {{ill|Daidō|en|Daidō|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daido}} ({{ill|807|en|807|WD=Q30016|lt=807 AD}}), a shrine building was constructed, and during the {{ill|Kōhei|en|Kōhei|WD=Q1193572|lt=Kōhei Era}} ({{ill|1058|en|1058|WD=Q19380|lt=1058 AD}} – {{ill|1065|en|1065|WD=Q19407|lt=1065 AD}}), {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|en|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|WD=Q3082851|lt=Minamoto no Yoriyoshi}} donated {{ill|Kanda|en|Kanda|WD=Q415424|lt=Kanda}}. Since the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period}}, the warrior families ({{ill|Buke (Japan)|de|Kriegerfamilien|ja|武家|lt=Buke|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buke (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=buke|WD=Q2927809}}) have shown strong devotion, and it is said that {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|en|Minamoto no Yoritomo|WD=Q242800|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo}} also made donations, with {{ill|Ashikaga Harushi|de|Ashikaga Harushi|ja|足利晴氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashikaga Harushi|1=Ashikaga Haruuji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11256588}} having offered a golden seal that still exists today.
In the 9th year of the {{ill|Eiroku|en|Eiroku|WD=Q1143341|lt=Eiroku Era}} ({{ill|1566|en|1566|WD=Q6638|lt=1566 AD}}), the shrine building was burned down in a battle between the {{ill|Kaijō Clan|de|Kaijō-Clan|ja|海上氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaijō Clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the {{ill|Awa Masaki Clan|de|Awa Masaki-Clan|ja|安房正木氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awa Masaki Clan|1=Awa Masaki Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450720}}. Although it was rebuilt during the {{ill|Genki (era)|en|Genki (era)|WD=Q1145064|lt=Genki Era}}, it was again burned down in the 1st year of the {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|WD=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era}} ({{ill|1573|en|1573|WD=Q6678|lt=1573 AD}}) and rebuilt in the following 2nd year of Tenshō ({{ill|1574|en|1574|WD=Q6680|lt=1574 AD}}). The current main hall was reconstructed in the 8th year of the {{ill|Enpō|en|Enpō|WD=Q1145735|lt=Enpō Era}} ({{ill|1680|en|1680|WD=Q7642|lt=1680 AD}}). Additionally, in the 19th year of Tenshō ({{ill|1591|en|1591|WD=Q6766|lt=1591 AD}}), {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|WD=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} donated 30 stones of {{ill|Red Seal Land|de|Rotsiegel-Land|ja|朱印地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Red Seal Land|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 朱印地・黒印地|1=shuinchi and kokuinchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11521520}}.
Every 60 years, in the {{ill|Kōshin Year|de|Kōshin-Jahr|ja|庚申|1=Kōshin|lt=Yang Metal Monkey|2=ja|3=庚申|4=zh|5=庚申|6=ko|7=경신|lt_ko=경신|8=fr|9=Singe de métal|10=de|11=Metall-Affe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin Year|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, the Shikinen Taisai Kamiyuki-sai is held.
== Cultural Properties == * One plaque from the annex of the Saruta Shrine main hall ({{ill|Chiba Prefecture-designated Tangible Cultural Property|de|Chiba-Präfektur-designierte greifbare Kulturimmobilie|ja|千葉県指定文化財一覧#建造物|lt=Chiba Prefecture-designated Tangible Cultural Property|lt_ja=千葉県指定有形文化財|lt_de=Chiba-Präfektur-designierte greifbare Kulturimmobilie|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiba Prefecture-designated Tangible Cultural Property|1=Chiba Prefecture-designated Tangible Cultural Property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406175}}) * The forest of Saruta Shrine ({{ill|Chiba Prefecture-designated Natural Monument|de|Chiba-Präfektur-designiertes Naturdenkmal|ja|千葉県指定文化財一覧#天然記念物|lt=Chiba Prefecture-designated Natural Monument|lt_ja=千葉県指定天然記念物|lt_de=Chiba-Präfektur-designiertes Naturdenkmal|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiba Prefecture-designated Natural Monument|1=Chiba Prefecture-designated Natural Monument|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406175}})
== Access == * 5-minute walk from {{ill|Saruda Station|en|Saruda Station|WD=Q5364199|lt=Saruta Station}} on the {{ill|Sōbu Main Line|en|Sōbu Main Line|WD=Q1191364|lt=Sōbu Main Line}}. * From either {{ill|Japan National Route 126|en|Japan National Route 126|WD=Q6156754|lt=National Route 126}} at Asahi City's Ato or {{ill|Japan National Route 356|en|Japan National Route 356|WD=Q6157325|lt=National Route 356}} at Choshi City's Nojiri, via the {{ill|Choshi-Asahi Line (Chiba Prefectural Road 71)|de|Choshi-Asahi-Linie (Chiba-Prefekturstraße 71)|ja|千葉県道71号銚子旭線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Choshi-Asahi Line (Chiba Prefectural Road 71)|1=Chiba Prefectural Road Route 71|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406653}}.
== Other == * There is a bridge called Sakushinbashi on the approach road that spans the JR {{ill|Sōbu Main Line|en|Sōbu Main Line|WD=Q1191364|lt=Sōbu Main Line}}. It was constructed by the railway company when the {{ill|Sōbu Railway (first generation)|de|Sōbu-Eisenbahn (Erste Generation)|ja|総武鉄道 (初代)|lt=Sōbu Railway|lt_ja=総武鉄道|lt_de=Sōbu Eisenbahn|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōbu Railway (first generation)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 総武本線|1=Sōbu Main Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1191364}} was extended to {{ill|Chōshi Station|en|Chōshi Station|WD=Q861084|lt=Choshi Station}} in {{ill|1897|en|1897|WD=Q7847|lt=1897 AD}} ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 30), which resulted in the approach road being divided. * Due to the remnants of {{ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|en|Shinbutsu-shūgō|WD=Q2279088|lt=Shinbutsu-shūgō}}, the shrine office is closed on Butsumetsu ({{ill|Butsumetsu (Buddhist calendar)|de|Butsumetsu|ja|六曜#仏滅|lt=Butsumetsu|lt_ja=仏滅|lt_de=Butsumetsu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Butsumetsu (Buddhist calendar)|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}) day.
== References == {{No footnotes|date=2020年2月|section=1}} * 『National Shrine Directory, Volume 1』, National Shrine Directory Publishing Society History Center, Showa 52. * 『Kadokawa Great Dictionary of Japanese Place Names, Volume 12: Chiba Prefecture』, Kadokawa Great Dictionary of Japanese Place Names Editorial Committee, Showa 59.
== External Links == * [http://www.choshikanko.com/extra/saruta/ Choshi City Saruta Shrine]
{{shinto-stub}}

Although it was rebuilt during the {{ill|Genki (era)|en|Genki (era)|WD=Q1145064|lt=Genki Era}}, it was again burned down in the 1st year of the {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|WD=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era}} ({{ill|1573|en|1573|WD=Q6678|lt=1573 AD}}) and rebuilt in the following 2nd year of Tenshō ({{ill|1574|en|1574|WD=Q6680|lt=1574 AD}}). It was constructed by the railway company when the {{ill|Sōbu Railway (first generation)|de|Sōbu-Eisenbahn (Erste Generation)|ja|総武鉄道 (初代)|lt=Sōbu Railway|lt_ja=総武鉄道|lt_de=Sōbu Eisenbahn|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōbu Railway (first generation)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 総武本線|1=Sōbu Main Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1191364}} was extended to {{ill|Chōshi Station|en|Chōshi Station|WD=Q861084|lt=Choshi Station}} in {{ill|1897|en|1897|WD=Q7847|lt=1897 AD}} ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 30), which resulted in the approach road being divided. * Due to the remnants of {{ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|en|Shinbutsu-shūgō|WD=Q2279088|lt=Shinbutsu-shūgō}}, the shrine office is closed on Butsumetsu ({{ill|Butsumetsu (Buddhist calendar)|de|Butsumetsu|ja|六曜#仏滅|lt=Butsumetsu|lt_ja=仏滅|lt_de=Butsumetsu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Butsumetsu (Buddhist calendar)|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}) day.

'''Saruta Shrine''' (猿田神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shrine}} located in Saruta-machi, {{ill|Chōshi|en|Chōshi|WD=Q694317|lt=Choshi City}} of {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture}}. It has long been venerated for practices such as {{ill|Directional Deity|de|Richtgott|ja|方位神|1=Directional deity|lt=Eight-Directional Protection|lt_ja=八方除け|lt_de=Achtseitiger Schutz|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Directional Deity|13=User:Immanuelle/Directional deity|qq=}}. Its principal deities are {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|en|Sarutahiko Ōkami|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|en|Ame-no-Uzume|WD=Q462803|lt=Ame-no-Uzume}}, and {{ill|Kukurihime|en|Kukurihime|WD=Q11619891|lt=Kukuri-hime-no-Mikoto}}.
== History == According to shrine tradition, it was founded on November 25 in the 25th year of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin}}, and during the reign of {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū}}, it became a branch shrine of {{ill|Ikuta Shrine|en|Ikuta Shrine|WD=Q710086|lt=Ikuta Shrine}}. In the 2nd year of {{ill|Daidō|en|Daidō|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daido}} ({{ill|807|en|807|WD=Q30016|lt=807 AD}}), a shrine building was constructed, and during the {{ill|Kōhei|en|Kōhei|WD=Q1193572|lt=Kōhei Era}} ({{ill|1058|en|1058|WD=Q19380|lt=1058 AD}} – {{ill|1065|en|1065|WD=Q19407|lt=1065 AD}}), {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|en|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|WD=Q3082851|lt=Minamoto no Yoriyoshi}} donated {{ill|Kanda|en|Kanda|WD=Q415424|lt=Kanda}}. Since the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period}}, the warrior families ({{ill|Buke (Japan)|de|Kriegerfamilien|ja|武家|lt=Buke|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buke (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=buke|WD=Q2927809}}) have shown strong devotion, and it is said that {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|en|Minamoto no Yoritomo|WD=Q242800|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo}} also made donations, with {{ill|Ashikaga Harushi|de|Ashikaga Harushi|ja|足利晴氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashikaga Harushi|1=Ashikaga Haruuji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11256588}} having offered a golden seal that still exists today.
In the 9th year of the {{ill|Eiroku|en|Eiroku|WD=Q1143341|lt=Eiroku Era}} ({{ill|1566|en|1566|WD=Q6638|lt=1566 AD}}), the shrine building was burned down in a battle between the {{ill|Kaijō Clan|de|Kaijō-Clan|ja|海上氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaijō Clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the {{ill|Awa Masaki Clan|de|Awa Masaki-Clan|ja|安房正木氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awa Masaki Clan|1=Awa Masaki Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450720}}. Although it was rebuilt during the {{ill|Genki (era)|en|Genki (era)|WD=Q1145064|lt=Genki Era}}, it was again burned down in the 1st year of the {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|WD=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era}} ({{ill|1573|en|1573|WD=Q6678|lt=1573 AD}}) and rebuilt in the following 2nd year of Tenshō ({{ill|1574|en|1574|WD=Q6680|lt=1574 AD}}). The current main hall was reconstructed in the 8th year of the {{ill|Enpō|en|Enpō|WD=Q1145735|lt=Enpō Era}} ({{ill|1680|en|1680|WD=Q7642|lt=1680 AD}}). Additionally, in the 19th year of Tenshō ({{ill|1591|en|1591|WD=Q6766|lt=1591 AD}}), {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|WD=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} donated 30 stones of {{ill|Red Seal Land|de|Rotsiegel-Land|ja|朱印地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Red Seal Land|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 朱印地・黒印地|1=shuinchi and kokuinchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11521520}}.
Every 60 years, in the {{ill|Kōshin Year|de|Kōshin-Jahr|ja|庚申|1=Kōshin|lt=Yang Metal Monkey|2=ja|3=庚申|4=zh|5=庚申|6=ko|7=경신|lt_ko=경신|8=fr|9=Singe de métal|10=de|11=Metall-Affe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin Year|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, the Shikinen Taisai Kamiyuki-sai is held.
== Cultural Properties == * One plaque from the annex of the Saruta Shrine main hall ({{ill|Chiba Prefecture-designated Tangible Cultural Property|de|Chiba-Präfektur-designierte greifbare Kulturimmobilie|ja|千葉県指定文化財一覧#建造物|lt=Chiba Prefecture-designated Tangible Cultural Property|lt_ja=千葉県指定有形文化財|lt_de=Chiba-Präfektur-designierte greifbare Kulturimmobilie|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiba Prefecture-designated Tangible Cultural Property|1=Chiba Prefecture-designated Tangible Cultural Property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406175}}) * The forest of Saruta Shrine ({{ill|Chiba Prefecture-designated Natural Monument|de|Chiba-Präfektur-designiertes Naturdenkmal|ja|千葉県指定文化財一覧#天然記念物|lt=Chiba Prefecture-designated Natural Monument|lt_ja=千葉県指定天然記念物|lt_de=Chiba-Präfektur-designiertes Naturdenkmal|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiba Prefecture-designated Natural Monument|1=Chiba Prefecture-designated Natural Monument|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406175}})
== Access == * 5-minute walk from {{ill|Saruda Station|en|Saruda Station|WD=Q5364199|lt=Saruta Station}} on the {{ill|Sōbu Main Line|en|Sōbu Main Line|WD=Q1191364|lt=Sōbu Main Line}}. * From either {{ill|Japan National Route 126|en|Japan National Route 126|WD=Q6156754|lt=National Route 126}} at Asahi City's Ato or {{ill|Japan National Route 356|en|Japan National Route 356|WD=Q6157325|lt=National Route 356}} at Choshi City's Nojiri, via the {{ill|Choshi-Asahi Line (Chiba Prefectural Road 71)|de|Choshi-Asahi-Linie (Chiba-Prefekturstraße 71)|ja|千葉県道71号銚子旭線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Choshi-Asahi Line (Chiba Prefectural Road 71)|1=Chiba Prefectural Road Route 71|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406653}}.
== Other == * There is a bridge called Sakushinbashi on the approach road that spans the JR {{ill|Sōbu Main Line|en|Sōbu Main Line|WD=Q1191364|lt=Sōbu Main Line}}. It was constructed by the railway company when the {{ill|Sōbu Railway (first generation)|de|Sōbu-Eisenbahn (Erste Generation)|ja|総武鉄道 (初代)|lt=Sōbu Railway|lt_ja=総武鉄道|lt_de=Sōbu Eisenbahn|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōbu Railway (first generation)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 総武本線|1=Sōbu Main Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1191364}} was extended to {{ill|Chōshi Station|en|Chōshi Station|WD=Q861084|lt=Choshi Station}} in {{ill|1897|en|1897|WD=Q7847|lt=1897 AD}} ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 30), which resulted in the approach road being divided. * Due to the remnants of {{ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|en|Shinbutsu-shūgō|WD=Q2279088|lt=Shinbutsu-shūgō}}, the shrine office is closed on Butsumetsu ({{ill|Butsumetsu (Buddhist calendar)|de|Butsumetsu|ja|六曜#仏滅|lt=Butsumetsu|lt_ja=仏滅|lt_de=Butsumetsu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Butsumetsu (Buddhist calendar)|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}) day.
== References == {{No footnotes|date=2020年2月|section=1}} * 『National Shrine Directory, Volume 1』, National Shrine Directory Publishing Society History Center, Showa 52. * 『Kadokawa Great Dictionary of Japanese Place Names, Volume 12: Chiba Prefecture』, Kadokawa Great Dictionary of Japanese Place Names Editorial Committee, Showa 59.
== External Links == * [http://www.choshikanko.com/extra/saruta/ Choshi City Saruta Shrine]

'''Saruta Shrine''' (猿田神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Saruta-machi, {{ill|Chōshi|en|Chōshi|WD=Q694317|lt=Choshi City|1=Chōshi}} of {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture}}. It has long been venerated for practices such as {{ill|Directional Deity|de|Richtgott|ja|方位神|1=Directional deity|lt=Eight-Directional Protection|lt_ja=八方除け|lt_de=Achtseitiger Schutz|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Directional Deity|13=User:Immanuelle/Directional deity|qq=}}. Its principal deities are {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|en|Sarutahiko Ōkami|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|en|Ame-no-Uzume|WD=Q462803|lt=Ame-no-Uzume|1=Ame-no-Uzume}}, and {{ill|Kukurihime|en|Kukurihime|WD=Q11619891|lt=Kukuri-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Kukurihime}}.
== History == According to shrine tradition, it was founded on November 25 in the 25th year of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin|1=Emperor Suinin}}, and during the reign of {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}}, it became a branch shrine of {{ill|Ikuta Shrine|en|Ikuta Shrine|WD=Q710086|lt=Ikuta Shrine|1=Ikuta Shrine}}. In the 2nd year of {{ill|Daidō|en|Daidō|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daido|1=Daidō}} ({{ill|807|en|807|WD=Q30016|lt=807 AD|1=807}}), a shrine building was constructed, and during the {{ill|Kōhei|en|Kōhei|WD=Q1193572|lt=Kōhei Era|1=Kōhei}} ({{ill|1058|en|1058|WD=Q19380|lt=1058 AD|1=1058}} – {{ill|1065|en|1065|WD=Q19407|lt=1065 AD|1=1065}}), {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|en|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|WD=Q3082851|lt=Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|1=Minamoto no Yoriyoshi}} donated {{ill|Kanda|en|Kanda|WD=Q415424|lt=Kanda|1=Kanda}}. Since the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}}, the warrior families ({{ill|Buke (Japan)|de|Kriegerfamilien|ja|武家|lt=Buke|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buke (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Buke|WD=Q2927809}}) have shown strong devotion, and it is said that {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|en|Minamoto no Yoritomo|WD=Q242800|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|1=Minamoto no Yoritomo}} also made donations, with {{ill|Ashikaga Harushi|de|Ashikaga Harushi|ja|足利晴氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashikaga Harushi|1=Ashikaga Haruuji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11256588}} having offered a golden seal that still exists today.
In the 9th year of the {{ill|Eiroku|en|Eiroku|WD=Q1143341|lt=Eiroku Era|1=Eiroku}} ({{ill|1566|en|1566|WD=Q6638|lt=1566 AD|1=1566}}), the shrine building was burned down in a battle between the {{ill|Kaijō Clan|de|Kaijō-Clan|ja|海上氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaijō Clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the {{ill|Awa Masaki Clan|de|Awa Masaki-Clan|ja|安房正木氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awa Masaki Clan|1=Q11450720|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450720}}. Although it was rebuilt during the {{ill|Genki (era)|en|Genki (era)|WD=Q1145064|lt=Genki Era|1=Genki (era)}}, it was again burned down in the 1st year of the {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|WD=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} ({{ill|1573|en|1573|WD=Q6678|lt=1573 AD|1=1573}}) and rebuilt in the following 2nd year of Tenshō ({{ill|1574|en|1574|WD=Q6680|lt=1574 AD|1=1574}}). The current main hall was reconstructed in the 8th year of the {{ill|Enpō|en|Enpō|WD=Q1145735|lt=Enpō Era|1=Enpō}} ({{ill|1680|en|1680|WD=Q7642|lt=1680 AD|1=1680}}). Additionally, in the 19th year of Tenshō ({{ill|1591|en|1591|WD=Q6766|lt=1591 AD|1=1591}}), {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|WD=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} donated 30 stones of {{ill|Red Seal Land|de|Rotsiegel-Land|ja|朱印地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Red Seal Land|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 朱印地・黒印地|1=shuinchi and kokuinchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11521520}}.
Every 60 years, in the {{ill|Kōshin Year|de|Kōshin-Jahr|ja|庚申|1=Kōshin|lt=Yang Metal Monkey|2=ja|3=庚申|4=zh|5=庚申|6=ko|7=경신|lt_ko=경신|8=fr|9=Singe de métal|10=de|11=Metall-Affe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin Year|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, the Shikinen Taisai Kamiyuki-sai is held.
== Cultural Properties == * One plaque from the annex of the Saruta Shrine main hall ({{ill|Chiba Prefecture-designated Tangible Cultural Property|de|Chiba-Präfektur-designierte greifbare Kulturimmobilie|ja|千葉県指定文化財一覧#建造物|lt=Chiba Prefecture-designated Tangible Cultural Property|lt_ja=千葉県指定有形文化財|lt_de=Chiba-Präfektur-designierte greifbare Kulturimmobilie|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiba Prefecture-designated Tangible Cultural Property|1=Chiba Prefecture-designated Tangible Cultural Property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406175}}) * The forest of Saruta Shrine ({{ill|Chiba Prefecture-designated Natural Monument|de|Chiba-Präfektur-designiertes Naturdenkmal|ja|千葉県指定文化財一覧#天然記念物|lt=Chiba Prefecture-designated Natural Monument|lt_ja=千葉県指定天然記念物|lt_de=Chiba-Präfektur-designiertes Naturdenkmal|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiba Prefecture-designated Natural Monument|1=Chiba Prefecture-designated Natural Monument|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406175}})
== Access == * 5-minute walk from {{ill|Saruda Station|en|Saruda Station|WD=Q5364199|lt=Saruta Station|1=Saruda Station}} on the {{ill|Sōbu Main Line|en|Sōbu Main Line|WD=Q1191364|lt=Sōbu Main Line|1=Sōbu Main Line}}. * From either {{ill|Japan National Route 126|en|Japan National Route 126|WD=Q6156754|lt=National Route 126|1=Japan National Route 126}} at Asahi City's Ato or {{ill|Japan National Route 356|en|Japan National Route 356|WD=Q6157325|lt=National Route 356|1=Japan National Route 356}} at Choshi City's Nojiri, via the {{ill|Choshi-Asahi Line (Chiba Prefectural Road 71)|de|Choshi-Asahi-Linie (Chiba-Prefekturstraße 71)|ja|千葉県道71号銚子旭線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Choshi-Asahi Line (Chiba Prefectural Road 71)|1=Chiba Prefectural Road Route 71|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406653}}.
== Other == * There is a bridge called Sakushinbashi on the approach road that spans the JR {{ill|Sōbu Main Line|en|Sōbu Main Line|WD=Q1191364|lt=Sōbu Main Line|1=Sōbu Main Line}}. It was constructed by the railway company when the {{ill|Sōbu Railway (first generation)|de|Sōbu-Eisenbahn (Erste Generation)|ja|総武鉄道 (初代)|lt=Sōbu Railway|lt_ja=総武鉄道|lt_de=Sōbu Eisenbahn|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōbu Railway (first generation)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 総武本線|1=Sōbu Main Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1191364}} was extended to {{ill|Chōshi Station|en|Chōshi Station|WD=Q861084|lt=Choshi Station|1=Chōshi Station}} in {{ill|1897|en|1897|WD=Q7847|lt=1897 AD|1=1897}} ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 30), which resulted in the approach road being divided. * Due to the remnants of {{ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|en|Shinbutsu-shūgō|WD=Q2279088|lt=Shinbutsu-shūgō|1=Shinbutsu-shūgō}}, the shrine office is closed on Butsumetsu ({{ill|Butsumetsu (Buddhist calendar)|de|Butsumetsu|ja|六曜#仏滅|lt=Butsumetsu|lt_ja=仏滅|lt_de=Butsumetsu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Butsumetsu (Buddhist calendar)|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}) day.
== References == {{No footnotes|date=2020年2月|section=1}} * 『National Shrine Directory, Volume 1』, National Shrine Directory Publishing Society History Center, Showa 52. * 『Kadokawa Great Dictionary of Japanese Place Names, Volume 12: Chiba Prefecture』, Kadokawa Great Dictionary of Japanese Place Names Editorial Committee, Showa 59.

'''Saruta Shrine''' (猿田神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Saruta-machi, {{ill|Chōshi|en|Chōshi|WD=Q694317|lt=Choshi City|1=Chōshi}} of {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture}}. It has long been venerated for practices such as {{ill|Directional Deity|de|Richtgott|ja|方位神|1=Directional deity|lt=Eight-Directional Protection|lt_ja=八方除け|lt_de=Achtseitiger Schutz|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Directional Deity|13=User:Immanuelle/Directional deity|qq=}}. Its principal deities are {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|en|Sarutahiko Ōkami|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|en|Ame-no-Uzume|WD=Q462803|lt=Ame-no-Uzume|1=Ame-no-Uzume}}, and {{ill|Kukurihime|en|Kukurihime|WD=Q11619891|lt=Kukuri-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Kukurihime}}.
== History == According to shrine tradition, it was founded on November 25 in the 25th year of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin|1=Emperor Suinin}}, and during the reign of {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}}, it became a branch shrine of {{ill|Ikuta Shrine|en|Ikuta Shrine|WD=Q710086|lt=Ikuta Shrine|1=Ikuta Shrine}}. In the 2nd year of {{ill|Daidō|en|Daidō|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daido|1=Daidō}} ({{ill|807|en|807|WD=Q30016|lt=807 AD|1=807}}), a shrine building was constructed, and during the {{ill|Kōhei|en|Kōhei|WD=Q1193572|lt=Kōhei Era|1=Kōhei}} ({{ill|1058|en|1058|WD=Q19380|lt=1058 AD|1=1058}} – {{ill|1065|en|1065|WD=Q19407|lt=1065 AD|1=1065}}), {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|en|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|WD=Q3082851|lt=Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|1=Minamoto no Yoriyoshi}} donated {{ill|Kanda|en|Kanda|WD=Q415424|lt=Kanda|1=Kanda}}. Since the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}}, the warrior families ({{ill|Buke (Japan)|de|Kriegerfamilien|ja|武家|lt=Buke|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buke (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Buke|WD=Q2927809}}) have shown strong devotion, and it is said that {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|en|Minamoto no Yoritomo|WD=Q242800|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|1=Minamoto no Yoritomo}} also made donations, with {{ill|Ashikaga Harushi|de|Ashikaga Harushi|ja|足利晴氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashikaga Harushi|1=Ashikaga Haruuji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11256588}} having offered a golden seal that still exists today.
In the 9th year of the {{ill|Eiroku|en|Eiroku|WD=Q1143341|lt=Eiroku Era|1=Eiroku}} ({{ill|1566|en|1566|WD=Q6638|lt=1566 AD|1=1566}}), the shrine building was burned down in a battle between the {{ill|Kaijō Clan|de|Kaijō-Clan|ja|海上氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaijō Clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the {{ill|Awa Masaki Clan|de|Awa Masaki-Clan|ja|安房正木氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awa Masaki Clan|1=Q11450720|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450720}}. Although it was rebuilt during the {{ill|Genki (era)|en|Genki (era)|WD=Q1145064|lt=Genki Era|1=Genki (era)}}, it was again burned down in the 1st year of the {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|WD=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} ({{ill|1573|en|1573|WD=Q6678|lt=1573 AD|1=1573}}) and rebuilt in the following 2nd year of Tenshō ({{ill|1574|en|1574|WD=Q6680|lt=1574 AD|1=1574}}). The current main hall was reconstructed in the 8th year of the {{ill|Enpō|en|Enpō|WD=Q1145735|lt=Enpō Era|1=Enpō}} ({{ill|1680|en|1680|WD=Q7642|lt=1680 AD|1=1680}}). Additionally, in the 19th year of Tenshō ({{ill|1591|en|1591|WD=Q6766|lt=1591 AD|1=1591}}), {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|WD=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} donated 30 stones of {{ill|Red Seal Land|de|Rotsiegel-Land|ja|朱印地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Red Seal Land|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 朱印地・黒印地|1=shuinchi and kokuinchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11521520}}.
Every 60 years, in the {{ill|Kōshin Year|de|Kōshin-Jahr|ja|庚申|1=Kōshin|lt=Yang Metal Monkey|2=ja|3=庚申|4=zh|5=庚申|6=ko|7=경신|lt_ko=경신|8=fr|9=Singe de métal|10=de|11=Metall-Affe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin Year|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, the Shikinen Taisai Kamiyuki-sai is held.
== Cultural Properties == * One plaque from the annex of the Saruta Shrine main hall ({{ill|Chiba Prefecture-designated Tangible Cultural Property|de|Chiba-Präfektur-designierte greifbare Kulturimmobilie|ja|千葉県指定文化財一覧#建造物|lt=Chiba Prefecture-designated Tangible Cultural Property|lt_ja=千葉県指定有形文化財|lt_de=Chiba-Präfektur-designierte greifbare Kulturimmobilie|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiba Prefecture-designated Tangible Cultural Property|1=Chiba Prefecture-designated Tangible Cultural Property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406175}}) * The forest of Saruta Shrine ({{ill|Chiba Prefecture-designated Natural Monument|de|Chiba-Präfektur-designiertes Naturdenkmal|ja|千葉県指定文化財一覧#天然記念物|lt=Chiba Prefecture-designated Natural Monument|lt_ja=千葉県指定天然記念物|lt_de=Chiba-Präfektur-designiertes Naturdenkmal|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiba Prefecture-designated Natural Monument|1=Chiba Prefecture-designated Natural Monument|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406175}})
== Access == * 5-minute walk from {{ill|Saruda Station|en|Saruda Station|WD=Q5364199|lt=Saruta Station|1=Saruda Station}} on the {{ill|Sōbu Main Line|en|Sōbu Main Line|WD=Q1191364|lt=Sōbu Main Line|1=Sōbu Main Line}}. * From either {{ill|Japan National Route 126|en|Japan National Route 126|WD=Q6156754|lt=National Route 126|1=Japan National Route 126}} at Asahi City's Ato or {{ill|Japan National Route 356|en|Japan National Route 356|WD=Q6157325|lt=National Route 356|1=Japan National Route 356}} at Choshi City's Nojiri, via the {{ill|Choshi-Asahi Line (Chiba Prefectural Road 71)|de|Choshi-Asahi-Linie (Chiba-Prefekturstraße 71)|ja|千葉県道71号銚子旭線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Choshi-Asahi Line (Chiba Prefectural Road 71)|1=Chiba Prefectural Road Route 71|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406653}}.
== Other == * There is a bridge called Sakushinbashi on the approach road that spans the JR {{ill|Sōbu Main Line|en|Sōbu Main Line|WD=Q1191364|lt=Sōbu Main Line|1=Sōbu Main Line}}. It was constructed by the railway company when the {{ill|Sōbu Railway (first generation)|de|Sōbu-Eisenbahn (Erste Generation)|ja|総武鉄道 (初代)|lt=Sōbu Railway|lt_ja=総武鉄道|lt_de=Sōbu Eisenbahn|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōbu Railway (first generation)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 総武本線|1=Sōbu Main Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1191364}} was extended to {{ill|Chōshi Station|en|Chōshi Station|WD=Q861084|lt=Choshi Station|1=Chōshi Station}} in {{ill|1897|en|1897|WD=Q7847|lt=1897 AD|1=1897}} ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 30), which resulted in the approach road being divided. * Due to the remnants of {{ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|en|Shinbutsu-shūgō|WD=Q2279088|lt=Shinbutsu-shūgō|1=Shinbutsu-shūgō}}, the shrine office is closed on Butsumetsu ({{ill|Butsumetsu (Buddhist calendar)|de|Butsumetsu|ja|六曜#仏滅|lt=Butsumetsu|lt_ja=仏滅|lt_de=Butsumetsu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Butsumetsu (Buddhist calendar)|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}) day.
== References == {{No footnotes|date=2020年2月|section=1}} * 『National Shrine Directory, Volume 1』, National Shrine Directory Publishing Society History Center, Showa 52. * 『Kadokawa Great Dictionary of Japanese Place Names, Volume 12: Chiba Prefecture』, Kadokawa Great Dictionary of Japanese Place Names Editorial Committee, Showa 59.
== External Links == * [http://www.choshikanko.com/extra/saruta/ Choshi City Saruta Shrine]

'''Saruta Shrine''' (猿田神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Saruta-machi, {{ill|Chōshi|en|Chōshi|WD=Q694317|lt=Choshi City|1=Chōshi}} of {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture}}. In the 2nd year of {{ill|Daidō|en|Daidō|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daido|1=Daidō}} ({{ill|807|en|807|WD=Q30016|lt=807 AD|1=807}}), a shrine building was constructed, and during the {{ill|Kōhei|en|Kōhei|WD=Q1193572|lt=Kōhei Era|1=Kōhei}} ({{ill|1058|en|1058|WD=Q19380|lt=1058 AD|1=1058}} – {{ill|1065|en|1065|WD=Q19407|lt=1065 AD|1=1065}}), {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|en|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|WD=Q3082851|lt=Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|1=Minamoto no Yoriyoshi}} donated {{ill|Kanda|en|Kanda|WD=Q415424|lt=Kanda|1=Kanda}}. Since the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}}, the warrior families ({{ill|Buke (Japan)|de|Kriegerfamilien|ja|武家|lt=Buke|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buke (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Buke|WD=Q2927809}}) have shown strong devotion, and it is said that {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|en|Minamoto no Yoritomo|WD=Q242800|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|1=Minamoto no Yoritomo}} also made donations, with {{ill|Ashikaga Harushi|de|Ashikaga Harushi|ja|足利晴氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashikaga Harushi|1=Ashikaga Haruuji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11256588}} having offered a golden seal that still exists today.
In the 9th year of the {{ill|Eiroku|en|Eiroku|WD=Q1143341|lt=Eiroku Era|1=Eiroku}} ({{ill|1566|en|1566|WD=Q6638|lt=1566 AD|1=1566}}), the shrine building was burned down in a battle between the {{ill|Kaijō Clan|de|Kaijō-Clan|ja|海上氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaijō Clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the {{ill|Awa Masaki Clan|de|Awa Masaki-Clan|ja|安房正木氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awa Masaki Clan|1=Q11450720|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450720}}. Although it was rebuilt during the {{ill|Genki (era)|en|Genki (era)|WD=Q1145064|lt=Genki Era|1=Genki (era)}}, it was again burned down in the 1st year of the {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|WD=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} ({{ill|1573|en|1573|WD=Q6678|lt=1573 AD|1=1573}}) and rebuilt in the following 2nd year of Tenshō ({{ill|1574|en|1574|WD=Q6680|lt=1574 AD|1=1574}}). The current main hall was reconstructed in the 8th year of the {{ill|Enpō|en|Enpō|WD=Q1145735|lt=Enpō Era|1=Enpō}} ({{ill|1680|en|1680|WD=Q7642|lt=1680 AD|1=1680}}). Additionally, in the 19th year of Tenshō ({{ill|1591|en|1591|WD=Q6766|lt=1591 AD|1=1591}}), {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|WD=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} donated 30 stones of {{ill|Red Seal Land|de|Rotsiegel-Land|ja|朱印地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Red Seal Land|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 朱印地・黒印地|1=shuinchi and kokuinchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11521520}}.
Every 60 years, in the {{ill|Kōshin Year|de|Kōshin-Jahr|ja|庚申|1=Kōshin|lt=Yang Metal Monkey|2=ja|3=庚申|4=zh|5=庚申|6=ko|7=경신|lt_ko=경신|8=fr|9=Singe de métal|10=de|11=Metall-Affe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin Year|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, the Shikinen Taisai Kamiyuki-sai is held.

'''Saruta Shrine''' (猿田神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Saruta-machi, {{ill|Chōshi|en|Chōshi|WD=Q694317|lt=Choshi City|1=Chōshi}} of {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture}}. It has long been venerated for practices such as {{ill|Directional Deity|de|Richtgott|ja|方位神|1=Directional deity|lt=Eight-Directional Protection|lt_ja=八方除け|lt_de=Achtseitiger Schutz|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Directional Deity|13=User:Immanuelle/Directional deity|qq=}}. Its principal deities are {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|en|Sarutahiko Ōkami|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|en|Ame-no-Uzume|WD=Q462803|lt=Ame-no-Uzume|1=Ame-no-Uzume}}, and {{ill|Kukurihime|en|Kukurihime|WD=Q11619891|lt=Kukuri-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Kukurihime}}.
== History == According to shrine tradition, it was founded on November 25 in the 25th year of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin|1=Emperor Suinin}}, and during the reign of {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}}, it became a branch shrine of {{ill|Ikuta Shrine|en|Ikuta Shrine|WD=Q710086|lt=Ikuta Shrine|1=Ikuta Shrine}}. In the 2nd year of {{ill|Daidō|en|Daidō|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daido|1=Daidō}} ({{ill|807|en|807|WD=Q30016|lt=807 AD|1=807}}), a shrine building was constructed, and during the {{ill|Kōhei|en|Kōhei|WD=Q1193572|lt=Kōhei Era|1=Kōhei}} ({{ill|1058|en|1058|WD=Q19380|lt=1058 AD|1=1058}} – {{ill|1065|en|1065|WD=Q19407|lt=1065 AD|1=1065}}), {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|en|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|WD=Q3082851|lt=Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|1=Minamoto no Yoriyoshi}} donated {{ill|Kanda|en|Kanda|WD=Q415424|lt=Kanda|1=Kanda}}. Since the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}}, the warrior families ({{ill|Buke (Japan)|de|Kriegerfamilien|ja|武家|lt=Buke|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buke (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Buke|WD=Q2927809}}) have shown strong devotion, and it is said that {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|en|Minamoto no Yoritomo|WD=Q242800|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|1=Minamoto no Yoritomo}} also made donations, with {{ill|Ashikaga Harushi|de|Ashikaga Harushi|ja|足利晴氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashikaga Harushi|1=Ashikaga Haruuji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11256588}} having offered a golden seal that still exists today.
In the 9th year of the {{ill|Eiroku|en|Eiroku|WD=Q1143341|lt=Eiroku Era|1=Eiroku}} ({{ill|1566|en|1566|WD=Q6638|lt=1566 AD|1=1566}}), the shrine building was burned down in a battle between the {{ill|Kaijō Clan|de|Kaijō-Clan|ja|海上氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaijō Clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the {{ill|Awa Masaki Clan|de|Awa Masaki-Clan|ja|安房正木氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awa Masaki Clan|1=Q11450720|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450720}}. Although it was rebuilt during the {{ill|Genki (era)|en|Genki (era)|WD=Q1145064|lt=Genki Era|1=Genki (era)}}, it was again burned down in the 1st year of the {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|WD=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} ({{ill|1573|en|1573|WD=Q6678|lt=1573 AD|1=1573}}) and rebuilt in the following 2nd year of Tenshō ({{ill|1574|en|1574|WD=Q6680|lt=1574 AD|1=1574}}). The current main hall was reconstructed in the 8th year of the {{ill|Enpō|en|Enpō|WD=Q1145735|lt=Enpō Era|1=Enpō}} ({{ill|1680|en|1680|WD=Q7642|lt=1680 AD|1=1680}}). Additionally, in the 19th year of Tenshō ({{ill|1591|en|1591|WD=Q6766|lt=1591 AD|1=1591}}), {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|WD=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} donated 30 stones of {{ill|Red Seal Land|de|Rotsiegel-Land|ja|朱印地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Red Seal Land|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 朱印地・黒印地|1=shuinchi and kokuinchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11521520}}.
Every 60 years, in the {{ill|Kōshin Year|de|Kōshin-Jahr|ja|庚申|1=Kōshin|lt=Yang Metal Monkey|2=ja|3=庚申|4=zh|5=庚申|6=ko|7=경신|lt_ko=경신|8=fr|9=Singe de métal|10=de|11=Metall-Affe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin Year|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, the Shikinen Taisai Kamiyuki-sai is held.
== Cultural Properties == * One plaque from the annex of the Saruta Shrine main hall ({{ill|Chiba Prefecture-designated Tangible Cultural Property|de|Chiba-Präfektur-designierte greifbare Kulturimmobilie|ja|千葉県指定文化財一覧#建造物|lt=Chiba Prefecture-designated Tangible Cultural Property|lt_ja=千葉県指定有形文化財|lt_de=Chiba-Präfektur-designierte greifbare Kulturimmobilie|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiba Prefecture-designated Tangible Cultural Property|1=Chiba Prefecture-designated Tangible Cultural Property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406175}}) * The forest of Saruta Shrine ({{ill|Chiba Prefecture-designated Natural Monument|de|Chiba-Präfektur-designiertes Naturdenkmal|ja|千葉県指定文化財一覧#天然記念物|lt=Chiba Prefecture-designated Natural Monument|lt_ja=千葉県指定天然記念物|lt_de=Chiba-Präfektur-designiertes Naturdenkmal|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiba Prefecture-designated Natural Monument|1=Chiba Prefecture-designated Natural Monument|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406175}})
== Access == * 5-minute walk from {{ill|Saruda Station|en|Saruda Station|WD=Q5364199|lt=Saruta Station|1=Saruda Station}} on the {{ill|Sōbu Main Line|en|Sōbu Main Line|WD=Q1191364|lt=Sōbu Main Line|1=Sōbu Main Line}}. * From either {{ill|Japan National Route 126|en|Japan National Route 126|WD=Q6156754|lt=National Route 126|1=Japan National Route 126}} at Asahi City's Ato or {{ill|Japan National Route 356|en|Japan National Route 356|WD=Q6157325|lt=National Route 356|1=Japan National Route 356}} at Choshi City's Nojiri, via the {{ill|Choshi-Asahi Line (Chiba Prefectural Road 71)|de|Choshi-Asahi-Linie (Chiba-Prefekturstraße 71)|ja|千葉県道71号銚子旭線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Choshi-Asahi Line (Chiba Prefectural Road 71)|1=Chiba Prefectural Road Route 71|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406653}}.
== Other == * There is a bridge called Sakushinbashi on the approach road that spans the JR {{ill|Sōbu Main Line|en|Sōbu Main Line|WD=Q1191364|lt=Sōbu Main Line|1=Sōbu Main Line}}. It was constructed by the railway company when the {{ill|Sōbu Railway (first generation)|de|Sōbu-Eisenbahn (Erste Generation)|ja|総武鉄道 (初代)|lt=Sōbu Railway|lt_ja=総武鉄道|lt_de=Sōbu Eisenbahn|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōbu Railway (first generation)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 総武本線|1=Sōbu Main Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1191364}} was extended to {{ill|Chōshi Station|en|Chōshi Station|WD=Q861084|lt=Choshi Station|1=Chōshi Station}} in {{ill|1897|en|1897|WD=Q7847|lt=1897 AD|1=1897}} ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 30), which resulted in the approach road being divided. * Due to the remnants of {{ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|en|Shinbutsu-shūgō|WD=Q2279088|lt=Shinbutsu-shūgō|1=Shinbutsu-shūgō}}, the shrine office is closed on Butsumetsu ({{ill|Butsumetsu (Buddhist calendar)|de|Butsumetsu|ja|六曜#仏滅|lt=Butsumetsu|lt_ja=仏滅|lt_de=Butsumetsu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Butsumetsu (Buddhist calendar)|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}) day.
== References == {{No footnotes|date=2020年2月|section=1}} * 『National Shrine Directory, Volume 1』, National Shrine Directory Publishing Society History Center, Showa 52. * 『Kadokawa Great Dictionary of Japanese Place Names, Volume 12: Chiba Prefecture』, Kadokawa Great Dictionary of Japanese Place Names Editorial Committee, Showa 59.
